name: Merlion Park
description: Singapore's most iconic landmark featuring the Merlion statue spouting water into Marina Bay. Spectacular views of Marina Bay Sands and the CBD skyline make this the most photographed spot in Singapore.

name: Marina Bay Sands – SkyPark Observation Deck
description: The iconic rooftop observation deck atop Marina Bay Sands offers a 360-degree panorama of Singapore's skyline, the Strait of Malacca, and the Southern Islands. The infinity pool here is one of the world's most famous.

name: ArtScience Museum
description: A lotus-shaped museum at Marina Bay blending art, science, culture, and technology through world-class permanent and travelling exhibitions. Home to the Future World permanent digital art gallery by teamLab.

name: Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay
description: Singapore's premier performing arts centre nicknamed the 'Durian' for its distinctive spiky dome design. Hosts world-class theatre, music, and dance performances with free outdoor concerts on the waterfront.

name: Gardens by the Bay – Supertree Grove
description: An otherworldly grove of 18 tree-like vertical gardens towering up to 16 storeys high, covered in tropical ferns and orchids. The nightly Garden Rhapsody light-and-sound show at 7:45 PM and 8:45 PM is unmissable.

name: Gardens by the Bay – Flower Dome
description: The world's largest glass greenhouse conservatory preserving plants from cool dry climates including ancient olive trees, baobabs, and rotating floral displays. Air-conditioned and perfect for Singapore's humid weather.

name: Gardens by the Bay – Cloud Forest
description: A misty mountain in a glass dome showcasing tropical highland plants and the world's tallest indoor waterfall at 35 metres. The cloud walk offers a spectacular aerial view of the lush vegetation below.

name: Gardens by the Bay – OCBC Skyway
description: A 128-metre aerial walkway connecting two Supertrees at 22 metres above ground, offering unparalleled bird's-eye views of the Supertree Grove, Marina Bay Sands, and the city skyline.

name: Gardens by the Bay – Satay by the Bay
description: An open-air hawker centre beside the Marina Reservoir at Gardens by the Bay, famous for satay and seafood with stunning water views. Enjoy charcoal-grilled satay sticks while gazing at the Supertrees.

name: Singapore Flyer
description: Asia's largest observation wheel at 165 metres, offering 360-degree views of Marina Bay, Sentosa, and even Malaysia and Indonesia on clear days. The 30-minute capsule ride is perfect at sunset.

name: Marina Barrage
description: Singapore's first reservoir in the city, with a rooftop green lawn perfect for kite flying and picnicking. The Sustainable Singapore Gallery inside educates visitors about water and urban solutions.

name: The Helix Bridge
description: An award-winning pedestrian bridge inspired by the DNA double-helix structure, connecting Marina Centre to Marina Bay Sands. Beautifully lit at night with four viewing platforms offering panoramic city views.

name: National Gallery Singapore
description: Housed in two restored national monuments — City Hall and the former Supreme Court — the gallery holds the world's largest public collection of Singapore and Southeast Asian modern art across 64,000 sq metres.

name: Victoria Theatre & Concert Hall
description: Singapore's oldest purpose-built theatre built in 1862, beautifully restored as a National Monument. The historic Victoria Concert Hall is home to the Singapore Symphony Orchestra.

name: Asian Civilisations Museum
description: Singapore's premier museum of Asian heritage housed in the historic Empress Place Building, featuring artefacts spanning 3,000 years of Asian art and culture from China, Southeast Asia, South Asia, and the Islamic world.

name: The Arts House (Old Parliament House)
description: Singapore's oldest government building (1827), now transformed into a dynamic arts venue with galleries, performing arts spaces, and a rooftop bar. An architectural gem of the colonial era.

name: The Fullerton Hotel Singapore
description: A National Monument built in 1928 as Singapore's main post office, now transformed into a five-star luxury hotel. The Palladian neoclassical architecture and waterfront location make it one of Singapore's most iconic buildings.

name: Lau Pa Sat – Festival Market (Indoor)
description: A historic Victorian cast-iron hawker centre in the heart of the CBD, gazetted as a National Monument. This beloved landmark serves local favourites like Hokkien mee, char kway teow, and satay to office workers and tourists alike.

name: Lau Pa Sat – Satay Street (Outdoor)
description: After dark, Boon Tat Street transforms into Singapore's most atmospheric open-air satay street, with rows of smoky charcoal grills, cold Tiger beer, and the buzz of the financial district crowd.

name: Rasapura Masters (Marina Bay Sands)
formatted_address: 2 Bayfront Avenue, B2-50 The Shoppes at MBS, Singapore 018972
description: A hawker-style food court inside Marina Bay Sands featuring curated local Singaporean dishes from renowned stalls, from chicken rice to laksa. Convenient, air-conditioned, and centrally located.

name: CÉ LA VI Restaurant & Sky Bar
description: A glamorous rooftop restaurant and sky bar perched on Level 57 of Marina Bay Sands offering spectacular panoramic city views. Renowned for its Asian-fusion cuisine and creative cocktails at sunset.

name: The Shoppes at Marina Bay Sands
description: Singapore's most glamorous luxury shopping mall with a 100-metre indoor gondola canal and over 300 world-class brands including Chanel, Louis Vuitton, and Dior. Home to the Celebrity Chef restaurants and a casino.

name: Marina Square
description: A large waterfront shopping complex near the Esplanade with over 200 retailers, restaurants, and a cinema. Connected to Suntec City and Millenia Walk via underground linkways and sheltered bridges.

name: Suntec City
description: One of Singapore's largest integrated shopping and convention centres, home to the Fountain of Wealth (once the world's largest fountain), over 300 stores, and major tech events at the Singapore Expo halls.

name: Millenia Walk
description: A sophisticated lifestyle mall connected to Conrad Centennial and Ritz-Carlton hotels near Marina Bay, known for its curated mix of home décor, lifestyle boutiques, and restaurants.

name: Raffles City Shopping Centre
description: A mega-complex above City Hall MRT station integrating a shopping mall, two luxury hotels (Fairmont and Swissôtel), and a convention centre. Over 200 retail outlets, F&B, and direct MRT access.

name: Boat Quay
description: A row of restored shophouses along the Singapore River brimming with al fresco restaurants, bars, and clubs. Once the commercial heart of colonial Singapore, Boat Quay now buzzes with nightlife and riverside dining.

name: Cavenagh Bridge
description: Singapore's oldest suspension bridge built in 1869 and a National Monument, spanning the Singapore River between Empress Place and the south bank. A beloved pedestrian walkway with scenic river views.

name: One Raffles Place Shopping Mall
description: A convenient underground mall beneath the iconic One Raffles Place skyscraper in the heart of the CBD, packed with food options, banks, and retail targeting the lunchtime office crowd.

name: Suntec City – Fountain of Wealth
description: One of the world's largest bronze fountains at the heart of Suntec City, symbolising the open palm of a hand bestowing wealth. Locals circle the fountain three times while touching the water for good luck.

name: Raffles Hotel Singapore
description: The grande dame of Singapore hotels, open since 1887. Raffles Hotel is a National Monument where the Singapore Sling cocktail was invented at the Long Bar. The restored colonial complex houses boutiques, restaurants, and museum.

name: Sir Stamford Raffles Landing Site
description: The spot on the north bank of the Singapore River where Sir Stamford Raffles is believed to have first landed in January 1819, marked by a white polymarble statue. A historic and photogenic riverside landmark.

name: Old Hill Street Police Station
description: A six-storey colonial building with 927 rainbow-coloured window shutters, now housing government arts agencies including MCI and MICA. The technicolour façade is a beloved photography landmark in Singapore.

name: Cathedral of the Good Shepherd
description: Singapore's oldest Roman Catholic cathedral (consecrated 1847) and a National Monument, designed in a Palladian style. A serene heritage landmark in the heart of the Bras Basah-Bugis arts district.

name: St Andrew's Cathedral
description: A stunning white Anglican cathedral completed in 1862 using a lime plaster made from eggshells and sugar, a National Monument. Set in lush grounds near City Hall MRT, it is Singapore's most iconic church.

name: CHIJMES
description: A stunning 19th-century Gothic chapel complex transformed into a premier dining and events destination. The hallowed halls and cobblestone courtyard of the former Convent of the Holy Infant Jesus now house some of Singapore's best restaurants and bars.

name: Battlebox (Fort Canning)
formatted_address: Cox Terrace, Fort Canning Park, Singapore 179616
description: The underground WWII command centre where General Percival made the fateful decision to surrender Singapore to Japan in 1942. The bunker has been restored as an immersive museum with wax figures and period artefacts.

name: Hong Lim Park (Speakers' Corner)
description: Singapore's only official free-speech area and Speakers' Corner, gazetted as a public green space since 1877. A shaded park in Chinatown popular for morning tai chi and occasional public demonstrations.

name: Telok Ayer Street Heritage Trail
description: A UNESCO-recognised street lined with three of Singapore's oldest places of worship — Thian Hock Keng Temple, Masjid Jamae, and Al-Abrar Mosque — reflecting the diverse religious history of early Singapore immigrants.

name: Nagore Dargah (Nagore Durgha Shrine)
description: One of Singapore's oldest Islamic shrines built between 1828 and 1830, dedicated to a Muslim saint from South India. Now also houses the Indian Muslim Heritage Centre with artefacts on early Indian Muslim migration.

name: Masjid Jamae (Chulia)
description: One of Singapore's oldest mosques, built in 1826 by South Indian Chulia Muslims, featuring a distinctive Neoclassical gateway contrasting with traditional Islamic interiors. A National Monument in Chinatown.

name: ION Orchard
description: The crown jewel of Orchard Road, this futuristic eight-storey mall integrates seamlessly with Orchard MRT and features over 300 luxury and mid-range brands, gourmet dining, and an ION Art gallery on Level 4.

name: Ngee Ann City (Takashimaya)
description: One of Orchard Road's grandest malls anchored by Takashimaya department store, with a vast basement supermarket, a renowned Japanese food hall, Crystal Jade, and the famous BookFest event every year.

name: Paragon
description: A prestigious Orchard Road mall housing Singapore's most concentrated collection of luxury brands including Gucci, Miu Miu, and Salvatore Ferragamo, along with Din Tai Fung and a Marks & Spencer.

name: 313@Somerset
description: A youth-oriented mall directly above Somerset MRT, popular with teenagers and young adults for its trendy fashion brands (Zara, Uniqlo, H&M), food options, and basement supermarket Cold Storage.

name: Orchard Central
description: Orchard Road's most eclectic mall known for its vertical garden wall and diverse mix of specialty retailers, niche F&B tenants, and an ice skating rink. Multiple outdoor terraces provide fresh air shopping breaks.

name: Far East Plaza
description: A Scotts Road institution popular for affordable fashion, tailoring shops, hair salons, and local eateries, beloved by budget-conscious shoppers and young fashion hunters since 1982.

name: Lucky Plaza
description: A busy, no-frills Orchard Road mall popular with Filipino domestic workers on their day off. Known for money changers, electronics, food court, and a unique cultural atmosphere every Sunday.

name: Wisma Atria
description: A heritage mall on Orchard Road directly connected to ION Orchard and Orchard MRT, featuring a bustling basement food court, a mix of fashion brands, and an al fresco dining street along Orchard Road.

name: Forum The Shopping Mall
description: A family-focused mall on upper Orchard Road specialising in children's brands (Toys R Us was formerly here) and premium baby goods. Now anchored by lifestyle and home brands.

name: Wheelock Place
description: A glass-pyramid mall on the corner of Orchard and Paterson Roads anchored by a flagship Borders bookstore until 2011, now home to Robinson's and specialty retailers. Iconic glass cone design on Orchard Road.

name: Shaw Centre
description: A compact Scotts Road mall featuring Marks & Spencer as an anchor tenant, plus a cinema, Japanese restaurant cluster, and Daiso lifestyle store. Conveniently connected to Shaw House.

name: Plaza Singapura
description: A family-friendly mall at the end of Orchard Road above Dhoby Ghaut MRT, anchored by UNIQLO, Best Denki, and Golden Village cinema. Known for affordable dining options and a wide food court.

name: The Centrepoint
description: A mid-range Orchard Road mall anchored by Robinson's department store and Cold Storage supermarket. Popular for its mix of fashion, beauty, and food outlets in the heart of Orchard Road.

name: Scotts Square
description: A premium boutique mall at the junction of Scotts and Orchard roads, featuring luxury brands and gourmet food options including Antoinette patisserie and Jaan restaurant. A serene alternative to busier Orchard malls.

name: Istana Singapore
description: The official residence and office of Singapore's President, set on a 100-acre landscaped estate built in 1869. The Istana grounds open to the public on selected public holidays for a rare glimpse of colonial grandeur.

name: Newton Food Centre
description: One of Singapore's most famous hawker centres and a regular fixture on 'best of Singapore' lists, with stalls serving charcoal-grilled sambal stingray, satay, chilli crab, and laksa around the clock.

name: Din Tai Fung (Paragon)
description: The world-famous Taiwanese dumpling restaurant renowned for its perfectly pleated xiao long bao (soup dumplings). The Paragon branch is one of Singapore's most popular, with queues worth the wait.

name: Food Republic (ION Orchard)
description: A retro-themed hawker-style food court on Level 4 of ION Orchard offering a wide variety of local and international cuisines including chicken rice, laksa, and Japanese dishes in a nostalgic Singapore setting.

name: Clarke Quay
description: A vibrant riverfront quay with five restored warehouse blocks turned into Singapore's premier entertainment precinct. Famous for nightclubs, al fresco restaurants, and a buzzing weekend night market vibe along the Singapore River.

name: Robertson Quay
description: A laid-back stretch of the Singapore River lined with trendy restaurants, wine bars, and brunch cafes in converted shophouses and low-rise waterfront buildings. Popular with expats and food lovers on weekends.

name: Fort Canning Park
description: A 18-hectare hilltop park with 700 years of Singapore history, featuring the 14th-century Keramat (ancient Malay shrine), WWII Battle Box bunker, Singapore's oldest Christian cemetery, and the outdoor concert venue.

name: Jumbo Seafood (Clarke Quay)
description: Singapore's most iconic chilli crab restaurant at Clarke Quay's Riverside Point, serving the definitive version of the national dish — mud crabs in a tangy, sweet-spicy chilli sauce, best eaten with mantou buns.

name: Great World
description: A riverside mall along the Singapore River near River Valley Road, offering a comfortable shopping experience with Cold Storage, NTUC FairPrice, major restaurants, and the Gold's Gym.

name: Zion Riverside Food Centre
description: A beloved neighbourhood hawker centre in River Valley known for its spectacular char kway teow and satay. One of Singapore's best-kept local secrets for old-school Singaporean cooking, popular with residents and foodies.

name: Sri Mariamman Temple
description: Singapore's oldest Hindu temple built in 1827 by Indian immigrants, a National Monument famous for its ornate Dravidian gopuram (tower) crowded with colourful statues of Hindu deities. The annual Thaipusam and Theemithi (firewalking) ceremonies are spectacular.

name: Thian Hock Keng Temple
description: Singapore's oldest and most important Hokkien temple dedicated to Ma Zu, goddess of the sea. Built in 1839 by early Chinese immigrants as an offering of thanks for safe sea passage. A UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Award winner.

name: Buddha Tooth Relic Temple & Museum
description: An imposing Buddhist temple built in the Tang Dynasty architectural style, housing a sacred Buddha tooth relic in a jewel-encrusted golden stupa. The rooftop garden, museum, and free vegetarian restaurant make this a complete experience.

name: Chinatown Heritage Centre
description: An immersive museum housed in three restored shophouses on Pagoda Street, recreating the cramped living conditions of early Chinese immigrants and the rich culture of Chinatown across different eras.

name: Chinatown Street Market (Pagoda & Trengganu St)
description: The heart of Singapore's Chinatown, with open-air stalls along Pagoda, Temple, and Trengganu Streets selling traditional handicrafts, souvenirs, silk goods, and festive decorations. Busiest during Chinese New Year.

name: Smith Street – Chinatown Food Street
description: An outdoor food street in Chinatown with umbrella-shaded tables and hawker-style stalls serving local classics like satay, BBQ stingray, prawn noodles, and laksa in an atmospheric alley setting.

name: Maxwell Food Centre
description: One of Singapore's most famous hawker centres in Chinatown, home to the legendary Tian Tian Hainanese Chicken Rice. Over 100 stalls serve everything from popiah to rojak, drawing long queues of locals and tourists alike.

name: Chinatown Complex Food Centre
description: Singapore's largest hawker centre with over 260 stalls spread across two floors, housing the world's cheapest Michelin-star meal — Chan Hon Meng's Soya Sauce Chicken. An essential stop for authentic local food.

name: Amoy Street Food Centre
description: A beloved two-storey hawker centre in the Telok Ayer conservation area packed with quality stalls, from fish soup and yong tau foo to bak chor mee and wanton noodles. Hugely popular with the CBD lunch crowd.

name: Hong Lim Market & Food Centre
description: A busy Chinatown hawker centre known for its outstanding popiah, char kway teow, and the famous Outram Park Ya Hua Rou Gu Cha (bak kut teh). A genuine local's favourite with a long heritage.

name: Tanjong Pagar Plaza Market & Food Centre
description: A comprehensive wet market and hawker centre at the base of Tanjong Pagar Plaza HDB, serving the mixed residential community with fresh produce, economy rice, Chinese pastries, and local breakfast favourites.

name: Chinatown Point
description: A conveniently located mall above Chinatown MRT with a diverse mix of retail outlets, a large food court, and a good selection of specialty Asian goods and lifestyle stores.

name: People's Park Complex
description: A Chinatown landmark since 1973, this mixed-use complex houses money changers, TCM shops, herbal medicine stores, tailors, and one of Singapore's best-known budget shopping floors for electronics and accessories.

name: People's Park Centre
description: Chinatown's other iconic shopping complex with an interesting mix of travel agencies, herbal products, goldsmith shops, and sundry retailers. A hub for the older Chinatown community.

name: Ann Siang Hill & Club Street
description: A charming enclave of restored conservation shophouses on a leafy hill in the CBD, housing independent boutiques, cosy bars, and gourmet restaurants. One of Singapore's most Instagram-worthy heritage streets.

name: Keong Saik Road
description: A rejuvenated street in Tanjong Pagar known for its trendy restaurants, rooftop bars, and boutique hotels in restored Art Deco shophouses. A favourite of Singapore's dining-out crowd and cocktail enthusiasts.

name: Tiong Bahru Market & Food Centre
description: Singapore's most beloved old-school hawker centre, serving the iconic neighbourhood of Tiong Bahru with exceptional stalls including famous Chai Chuan Tou Yang Rou Tang (mutton soup) and traditional kueh makers.

name: Tiong Bahru Plaza
description: The neighbourhood mall of the trendy Tiong Bahru estate, with a NTUC FairPrice supermarket, boutique bookshop (Books Actually nearby), cafes, and daily necessities. Surrounded by Singapore's first public housing estate.

name: Tiong Bahru Estate Heritage Walk
description: Singapore's oldest public housing estate from the 1930s, featuring art deco curved apartment blocks, charming ground-floor shops, murals, and independent cafes. A UNESCO-recognized pre-war heritage neighbourhood.

name: Eu Yan Sang Heritage Building
description: The oldest Eu Yan Sang traditional Chinese medicine clinic in Singapore, operating since 1910 in a heritage shophouse on South Bridge Road. The shop's distinctive red and gold interior embodies 100 years of TCM heritage in Singapore.

name: Baba House
description: A meticulously restored 1895 Peranakan shophouse museum managed by NUS, offering guided tours of authentic Nonya antiques, intricate tilework, and original family furniture. One of the best-preserved Peranakan homes in Southeast Asia.

name: Sultan Mosque (Masjid Sultan)
description: Singapore's largest and most stunning mosque, built in 1824 and gazetted as a National Monument. The golden dome, Moorish architecture, and serene prayer halls make this the spiritual heart of the Muslim Malay community in Singapore.

name: Masjid Hajjah Fatimah
description: A unique national monument mosque built in 1845 with an eclectic blend of Malay, European, and Chinese architectural styles. Notable for its minaret which leans slightly, earning it the nickname 'Singapore's Leaning Tower'.

name: Haji Lane & Arab Street
description: Singapore's hippest street — a narrow alley in Kampong Glam packed with bohemian boutiques, vintage stores, Middle Eastern cafes, and trendy bars in colourfully painted shophouses. Fantastic for street photography and window shopping.

name: Kampong Glam Heritage Trail
description: The historic Malay royal district around Sultan Mosque, with the restored Malay Heritage Centre in the former palace, textile shops on Arab Street, and the colourful murals and street art of Haji Lane.

name: Little India Arcade
description: A compact arcade of heritage shophouses opposite the Tekka Market selling everything Indian: saris, bangles, kolam powders, flower garlands, incense, Ayurvedic herbal products, and South Indian snacks.

name: Sri Veeramakaliamman Temple
description: Little India's most ornate and beloved Hindu temple dedicated to the fierce goddess Kali, featuring a spectacular 23-metre-tall seven-tiered rajagopuram tower adorned with colourful statues. Built by Bengali labourers in 1881.

name: Sri Srinivasa Perumal Temple
description: A magnificent National Monument Vaishnavite temple with one of Singapore's most elaborate gopurams (temple towers). The starting point of the annual Thaipusam procession that ends at Sri Mariamman Temple.

name: Sakya Muni Buddha Gaya Temple (1000 Lights)
description: A Thai Buddhist temple in Little India featuring a 15-metre-tall seated Buddha surrounded by 1,000 electric lights. Thai, Chinese, and Indian Buddhist traditions blend here in one of Singapore's most atmospheric temples.

name: Indian Heritage Centre
description: A purpose-built museum opened in 2015 to explore the origins and contributions of the Indian communities in Singapore, housing over 1,000 artefacts across four thematic galleries from ancient trade routes to the modern era.

name: Kwan Im Thong Hood Cho Temple
description: Singapore's most visited temple dedicated to Kuan Yin, the Goddess of Mercy. Thousands of devotees come daily to pray and shake fortune sticks (kau cim), especially on the 1st and 15th days of the Chinese lunar month.

name: Armenian Church of St Gregory the Illuminator
description: Singapore's oldest surviving Christian church, built in 1835 by the small but influential Armenian community. The perfect neo-classical circular church is a National Monument where the grave of Agnes Joaquim, who discovered Singapore's national flower (the Vanda Miss Joaquim orchid), is located.

name: Tekka Market (Zhu Jiao Centre)
description: Little India's famous wet market and hawker centre with a vibrant ground floor of fresh produce vendors, flowers, and Indian groceries, and an upper floor of hawker stalls serving South and North Indian dishes, mee goreng, and roti prata.

name: Albert Centre Market & Food Centre
description: A busy central hawker centre in the Bugis area packed with affordable local food stalls from Hokkien prawn mee and fishball noodles to popiah and carrot cake. Beloved by students from nearby LASALLE College.

name: Golden Mile Food Centre
description: A basement hawker centre in the Golden Mile Complex building, known for its outstanding lor mee, fried carrot cake, and nasi padang. Popular with Beach Road office workers and the Thai-Singaporean community.

name: Berseh Food Centre
description: A popular Jalan Besar hawker centre known for exceptional prawn noodles, wanton mee, and Teochew porridge. A neighbourhood gem that attracts a loyal following of local foodies seeking old-school Singapore flavours.

name: Bugis Junction
description: A unique mall built over restored Victorian-era shophouses with glass-covered streets, directly linked to Bugis MRT. Popular with younger shoppers for its trendy fashion labels, Japanese F&B, and cinema.

name: Bugis+
description: Connected to Bugis Junction via a bridge, this youth-oriented mall features niche street fashion brands, bubble tea shops, boba cafes, and gaming stores popular with teenagers and young adults.

name: Mustafa Centre
description: A legendary 24-hour, 6-storey department store in Little India selling everything from electronics and gold jewellery to groceries, perfumes, saris, and international food products. A one-stop shop that never closes.

name: Sim Lim Square
description: Singapore's most famous electronics mall with six floors of camera equipment, mobile phones, computers, audio systems, and electronic accessories. A destination for tech enthusiasts, though bargaining is recommended.

name: Golden Mile Complex
description: A brutalist-style 1973 iconic building gazetted for conservation, long known as 'Little Thailand' for its Thai restaurants, Thai grocery shops, and Bangkok bus booking services. Now being repurposed while retaining heritage.

name: City Square Mall
description: Singapore's first eco-friendly mall and a green building icon, located above Farrer Park MRT. Features a large NTUC FairPrice supermarket, Isetan department store, and a wide range of family-oriented F&B outlets.

name: National Library Singapore (Lee Kong Chian Reference Library)
description: Singapore's principal public library, a 16-storey building with over 1 million items in its collection. Features the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library with rare Singapore and Southeast Asia materials, plus a rooftop art gallery.

name: Singapore Art Museum (SAM)
description: Housed in a restored 19th-century Catholic mission school building, SAM is dedicated to contemporary art from Singapore and Southeast Asia, with rotating exhibitions, an outdoor sculpture garden, and a rooftop café.

name: Peranakan Museum
description: The world's finest museum dedicated to Peranakan (Straits Chinese) culture, housed in the former Tao Nan School. Stunning collections of Nonya kebayas, intricate beadwork, fine porcelain, and wedding artefacts.

name: National Museum of Singapore
description: Singapore's oldest museum (founded 1887) in a magnificent Neo-Palladian building with a modern glass rotunda extension. The permanent Singapore History Gallery traces the nation's story from 14th-century Singapura to modern independence.

name: Universal Studios Singapore
description: Southeast Asia's first and only Universal Studios theme park with seven themed zones including Hollywood, Sci-Fi City, Jurassic World, and the Minion Land. Features 24 rides and shows across 49 acres of thrills and entertainment.

name: S.E.A. Aquarium (Singapore Oceanarium)
description: One of the world's largest aquariums housing over 100,000 marine animals across 50 habitats. The 36-metre-wide Open Ocean viewing panel is the world's largest single aquarium viewing panel, offering a breathtaking underwater panorama.

name: Adventure Cove Waterpark
description: A massive outdoor waterpark at Resorts World Sentosa with thrilling water slides, a 620-metre Adventure River, a wave pool, and the unique Ray Bay where visitors snorkel alongside rays and tropical fish.

name: Wings of Time
description: An award-winning nightly outdoor spectacle combining water fountains, laser projections, fire effects, and fireworks set to original music at Palawan Beach. A breathtaking 25-minute show that can be seen from the beach.

name: iFLY Singapore
description: The world's largest themed wind tunnel providing an indoor skydiving experience for all ages and skill levels. Feel the sensation of freefall at 200 km/h in a safe and controlled environment with certified instructors.

name: Madame Tussauds Singapore
description: Singapore's celebrity wax museum featuring life-like figures of global icons from Taylor Swift to Lee Kuan Yew, plus a Marvel Superheroes 4D cinema experience and a unique Images of Singapore LIVE section.

name: Siloso Beach, Sentosa
description: Sentosa's most vibrant and popular beach, stretching 1.5 km with volleyball nets, deckchair rentals, beach bars including Tanjong Beach Club, and stunning sunsets facing the Strait of Malacca and western shipping lanes.

name: Palawan Beach, Sentosa
description: A family-friendly beach in the heart of Sentosa, home to the Southernmost Point of Continental Asia suspension bridge. Features a massive children's adventure playground, water sports rentals, and the Wings of Time venue.

name: Tanjong Beach, Sentosa
description: Sentosa's most peaceful and picturesque beach, offering a retreat from the crowds with a gently curving bay, Tanjong Beach Club for sophisticated outdoor dining, and beautiful sunset views over the sea.

name: Fort Siloso
description: Singapore's only preserved WWII coastal fort, featuring an immersive walk-through of underground bunkers, gun batteries, and tunnels with life-like mannequin displays recreating scenes of the 1942 Japanese invasion and fall of Singapore.

name: Skyline Luge Sentosa
description: A thrilling gravity-powered go-kart ride winding down Sentosa's hillside on specially designed three-wheeled carts. Riders control their own speed on the 688-metre track before taking the chairlift back up for more runs.

name: Sentosa Boardwalk
description: A 670-metre landscaped boardwalk connecting VivoCity to Sentosa Island across a tranquil sea inlet, lined with lush greenery, misting fans, and art installations. The most scenic and free way to walk into Sentosa.

name: Sentosa Cable Car (Mount Faber Line)
description: A 1.5 km cable car route between Mount Faber, HarbourFront Tower, and Sentosa, offering panoramic aerial views of the southern islands and the Strait of Malacca. The Cable Car Sky Dining option serves meals in suspended gondolas.

name: Resorts World Sentosa – Casino
description: One of Singapore's two integrated resort casinos, open 24 hours to foreigners (Singapore residents pay a levy). Features over 500 gaming tables and 2,400 slot machines across four themed gaming areas.

name: Trick Eye Museum Sentosa
description: An interactive 3D optical illusion museum where visitors become part of the paintings for hilarious, share-worthy photos. With over 80 artworks across themed zones, it's a favourite for families and social-media lovers.

name: Tanjong Beach Club
description: Sentosa's most stylish beach club with a stunning infinity pool, imported white sand, and a restaurant serving Mediterranean and Asian fusion dishes. The Sunday beach parties are legendary on Singapore's social calendar.

name: The Knolls (Capella Singapore)
description: An award-winning restaurant within the grand Capella Singapore luxury resort, serving modern colonial cuisine with French and Asian influences in a stunning colonial-era colonial building with sea views.

name: Images of Singapore LIVE
description: An immersive walk-through attraction within Madame Tussauds telling Singapore's story through realistic recreations of hawker streets, kampongs, and key historical moments using vivid sensory exhibits.

name: East Coast Park
description: Singapore's largest and most popular park, stretching 15 km along the southeastern coastline. A beloved destination for cycling, rollerblading, barbecuing, beach volleyball, and camping, with a long lagoon beach and seafood restaurants.

name: East Coast Lagoon Food Village
description: A hugely popular beachside hawker centre with sea breezes and alfresco dining, famous for its satay, BBQ seafood, laksa, and satay bee hoon. A perfect pairing with a walk or cycle along East Coast Park.

name: Old Airport Road Food Centre
description: One of Singapore's oldest and most celebrated hawker centres, built on the former Kallang Airport site in 1973. Home to legendary stalls including Toa Payoh Rojak, Dong Ji Fried Kway Teow, and Min Ji Fried Carrot Cake.

name: Geylang Serai Market & Food Centre
description: The vibrant heart of Singapore's Malay community, with a wet market packed with herbs, spices, Malay kueh, and fresh produce downstairs, and a hawker centre serving nasi padang, mee siam, and Malay cuisine upstairs.

name: Bedok Interchange Hawker Centre
description: One of the busiest hawker centres in the East, directly above Bedok MRT and bus interchange. Famous for its diverse food options from Hokkien mee to Malay grills, drawing crowds day and night from the large Bedok residential population.

name: Bedok 85 Fengshan Market (Fengshan Centre)
description: A legendary open-air evening hawker centre in Bedok, famous for its outstanding BBQ sambal stingray, oyster omelette (orh jian), and satay bee hoon. Highly popular with late-night diners and food bloggers.

name: Changi Beach Park
description: A tranquil beachside park in the far east of Singapore with a gentle swimming lagoon, barbecue pits, and picnic areas. Offers views of the Riau Islands and a peaceful escape from the city, close to Changi Village.

name: Pasir Ris Park
description: A large northeastern Singapore park featuring a 6-hectare mangrove boardwalk, a beach, chalets, cycling tracks, and a water playground. A popular family destination especially for children's birthday parties and weekend barbecues.

name: Joo Chiat / Katong Peranakan Heritage
description: Singapore's best-preserved Peranakan neighbourhood with rows of ornately tiled terrace houses, Nonya cake shops, laksa stalls, and Peranakan restaurants along East Coast Road. The vivid architecture reflects Straits Chinese culture at its finest.

name: i12 Katong
description: A lifestyle mall in the heart of Katong neighbourhood with a NTUC FairPrice supermarket, restaurants, and family-oriented stores. An excellent base for exploring the heritage East Coast Road food scene.

name: Parkway Parade
description: The largest shopping mall in the Marine Parade area, serving the east Singapore community since 1984 with department stores, a cinema, supermarket, food court, and a wide variety of retail tenants.

name: Tampines Mall
description: The anchor mall in Tampines town centre above Tampines MRT, with major anchors including Isetan, a cinema complex, and a wide food court. A bustling community hub for one of Singapore's largest HDB towns.

name: Century Square
description: Connected to Tampines Mall via a link bridge, Century Square offers an extended shopping experience with additional F&B options, specialty stores, and a Cold Storage supermarket for east Singapore residents.

name: Changi City Point
description: A mid-sized mall serving Changi Business Park's IT and technology workforce, with office-friendly F&B, a Decathlon sports megastore, and convenient MRT access via Expo MRT on the East-West Line.

name: Singapore EXPO Convention & Exhibition Centre
description: Singapore's largest convention and exhibition centre, hosting major trade shows like IT Show, SITEX, Big Big Sale, and international conferences. It features 10 interconnected halls with a total capacity for 100,000 visitors.

name: Geylang Road Durian Belt
description: The most famous stretch in Singapore for buying and eating durian, often called the King of Fruits. The night market-like atmosphere along Lorong 1-10 of Geylang buzzes with durian sellers and their pungent, prized Musang King and D24 varieties.

name: East Coast Seafood Centre
description: A cluster of Singapore's most famous seafood restaurants along East Coast Park, including Long Beach Seafood and Jumbo Seafood. The place for chilli crab, black pepper crab, and drunken prawn with sea breezes.

name: Singapore Zoo
description: An award-winning rainforest zoo where animals roam in naturalistic open habitats separated by moats instead of cages. Home to over 300 species including white tigers, giraffes, and sun bears, with a breakfast-with-orangutans experience.

name: Night Safari
description: The world's first nocturnal wildlife park, allowing visitors to observe 2,500 animals from 130 species under moonlight. The Tram Ride loops 3.2 km through seven geographic zones while the Creatures of the Night show is unmissable.

name: River Wonders
description: Asia's first and largest river-themed wildlife park, showcasing freshwater habitats from the Amazon to the Yangtze. Home to giant river otters, Amazonian manatees, and Singapore's most-loved giant pandas Jia Jia and Le Le.

name: Bird Paradise
description: The world's largest bird park featuring over 3,500 birds across 400 species in eight walk-through aviaries including the Wings of Asia and African Waterfall aviaries. Opened in 2023 at the new Mandai Wildlife Reserve campus.

name: Mandai Wildlife Bridge
description: A world-first wildlife-themed road crossing built above Mandai Road to allow animals such as civets, pangolins, and wild boar to safely cross between the Central Catchment Nature Reserve and the Mandai forests.

name: Causeway Point
description: The largest shopping mall in the north, directly connected to Woodlands MRT and the Johor Causeway. A comprehensive one-stop destination with Golden Village cinema, NTUC FairPrice, and over 400 stores.

name: Northpoint City
description: Singapore's largest mall in the north, anchored by a NTUC FairPrice Extra, BHG department store, cinema, rooftop community club, and a sheltered bus interchange. Directly linked to Yishun MRT.

name: Sembawang Shopping Centre
description: A neighbourhood mall in the Sembawang estate offering a community mix of supermarket, wet market, food court, and retail stores serving the northern residential area and nearby naval base personnel.

name: Woodlands Waterfront Park
description: A scenic 11-hectare waterfront park at the northern tip of Singapore offering panoramic views of the Johor Strait and Malaysia across the water. Features a 393-metre pier jetty, cycling paths, and a playground.

name: Sembawang Park
description: A rustic coastal park on the Johor Strait with a historic wooden pier and jetty, fishing spots, sandy beach, and lush greenery. One of Singapore's last kampong-era parks, beloved for its old-world charm and tranquillity.

name: Chomp Chomp Food Centre
description: One of Singapore's most beloved outdoor evening hawker centres in Serangoon Gardens, drawing long queues for satay, BBQ sambal stingray, oyster omelette, and fried carrot cake. A favourite of locals and savvy tourists.

name: Serangoon Garden Market & Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre and wet market at the heart of the charming Serangoon Gardens estate. Famous for its well-loved wanton mee stall and diverse selection of breakfast and lunch options.

name: Chong Pang Market & Food Centre
description: A lively north Singapore hawker centre and wet market known for its excellent BBQ chicken wings, Malay nasi campur, and Chinese hawker classics. Busy from early morning through dinner with a loyal neighbourhood following.

name: Jurong Lake Gardens
description: Singapore's third national garden and the first in the heartlands, featuring the lakeside Grasslands, Clusia Cove, and a stunning Forest Ramble children's nature playground. The sunset over Jurong Lake is one of Singapore's finest.

name: Science Centre Singapore
description: Singapore's premier science museum with over 1,000 interactive exhibits across 14 galleries covering technology, space, life science, and physics. The Snow City ice playground and Omni-Theatre IMAX dome are adjacent highlights.

name: Chinese Garden (Yu Hwa Yuan)
description: A beautiful Chinese-style garden on Jurong Lake Island, modelled after Beijing's Summer Palace. Features pagodas, pavilions, bridges, and a turtle and tortoise museum. Currently undergoing enhancement as part of Jurong Lake Gardens.

name: Japanese Garden (Seiwaen)
description: One of Asia's largest Japanese-style gardens, featuring stone lanterns, koi ponds, teahouses, and Zen-inspired rock arrangements on a small island in Jurong Lake. Connected to the Chinese Garden by a decorative bridge.

name: Haw Par Villa
description: A bizarre and fascinating Chinese mythology theme park with over 1,000 statues and 150 dioramas depicting scenes from Chinese folklore, legends, and moral tales — including the famous Ten Courts of Hell. Admission is free.

name: West Coast Park
description: A popular western Singapore park with a large outdoor adventure playground (the MacDonald's playground), cycling tracks, barbecue pits, fishing jetty, and open lawns. A beloved family destination for west Singapore residents.

name: Labrador Nature Reserve & Park
description: A nature reserve and coastal park with WWII-era gun battery remnants, lush secondary rainforest, rocky shoreline, and a heritage trail. Offers rare views of the Singapore Strait and the Port of Singapore from the clifftops.

name: Reflections at Bukit Chandu
description: A WWII interpretive museum at Kent Ridge Park commemorating the heroic last stand of the Malay Regiment against Japanese forces on 14 February 1942. Housed in a restored colonial-era black-and-white bungalow with rare artefacts and multimedia exhibits.

name: Westgate
description: A premium lifestyle mall at Jurong East connected to JEM and IMM, featuring a rooftop terrace, kids' discovery zone, diverse dining options, and anchors including Robinsons, Marks & Spencer, and Golden Village cinema.

name: JEM (Jurong East Mall)
description: The largest mall in the west by floor area, anchored by NTUC FairPrice Finest, Marks & Spencer food hall, Old Chang Kee, and over 240 stores. Part of the Jurong East gateway hub directly above Jurong East MRT.

name: IMM
description: Singapore's largest outlet shopping mall, home to over 100 factory outlet stores offering brands at discounted prices, plus Singapore's most comprehensive IKEA store. A magnet for bargain hunters in the west.

name: Jurong Point
description: One of Singapore's largest suburban malls in the far west, connected to Boon Lay MRT and Jurong Point Bus Interchange. Features a comprehensive NTUC FairPrice Extra, Giant hypermarket, and over 400 retail stores.

name: The Clementi Mall
description: A seven-storey neighbourhood mall directly above Clementi MRT, offering a convenient mix of NTUC FairPrice, BHG department store, popular F&B chains, and a Golden Village cinema for west Singapore residents.

name: Buona Vista Market & Food Centre
description: A well-loved neighbourhood hawker centre in Holland Drive serving a mix of Chinese, Malay, and Indian dishes. Known for quality breakfast options including roti prata, nasi lemak, and ban mian (flat noodle soup).

name: Ghim Moh Market & Food Centre
description: A busy wet market and hawker centre in the Holland Village vicinity, famous for its outstanding kueh and nonya dishes including onde-onde, kueh lapis, and chye tow kway. A local favourite for traditional Peranakan kueh.

name: Clementi Market & Food Centre
description: A comprehensive wet market and hawker centre at the heart of Clementi town, offering fresh produce on the lower level and diverse hawker food including famous fishball noodles and prawn paste chicken on the upper level.

name: West Coast Drive Market & Food Centre
description: A relaxed hawker centre in the West Coast estate known for its quality fried Hokkien prawn mee and a loyal neighbourhood following. A peaceful spot away from tourist crowds with genuine local flavours.

name: VivoCity
description: Singapore's largest shopping mall with over 300 stores, a rooftop amphitheatre, a rooftop pool, and direct access to HarbourFront MRT and Sentosa Boardwalk. The cinema, food options, and rooftop views make it a full-day destination.

name: HarbourFront Centre
description: A waterfront complex adjacent to VivoCity housing the HarbourFront Ferry Terminal (ferries to Batam and Bintan), a retail podium, and ferry ticket offices. Gateway to Indonesian island getaways.

name: Mount Faber Park
description: One of Singapore's oldest parks at 105 metres above sea level, offering sweeping views of the Strait of Malacca, Sentosa, and the southern islands. The cable car station, Faber Point lookout, and Dusk Restaurant make it a romantic retreat.

name: Henderson Waves Bridge
description: Singapore's highest pedestrian bridge at 36 metres above Henderson Road, with an undulating wave-like structure containing sheltered alcoves. Part of the Southern Ridges walking trail connecting Mount Faber Park to Telok Blangah Hill Park.

name: Southern Ridges Trail
description: A 10-kilometre ridgeline trail connecting Kent Ridge Park, HortPark, Telok Blangah Hill, Henderson Waves, and Mount Faber. One of Singapore's best urban hikes with canopy walks, forest paths, and sea views.

name: HortPark
description: A gardening hub and lifestyle park along the Southern Ridges, featuring themed display gardens, water features, a children's garden, and Singapore's largest butterfly house. An ideal stop on the Southern Ridges hiking trail.

name: Kent Ridge Park
description: A forested hilltop park above the National University of Singapore, marking the site of a WWII battle in 1942. The Canopy Walk through the rainforest canopy and ocean views from the ridge are the park's highlights.

name: Telok Blangah Hill Park
description: A forested urban park on the Southern Ridges connecting HortPark to Henderson Waves, with shaded walking trails, picnic meadows, and a community garden. A cool green retreat with views over Keppel Harbour.

name: Pasir Panjang Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre near NUS known for its excellent zi char (Chinese cook-to-order dishes), sambal stingray, and fried kway teow. Popular with students and researchers from the nearby National University of Singapore.

name: Alexandra Village Food Centre
description: A cherished hawker centre in Alexandra housing the famous Ah Kow Mushroom Minced Pork Mee and outstanding prawn paste chicken. Known for consistent quality across stalls and a loyal neighbourhood fanbase.

name: ABC Brickworks Market & Food Centre
description: A beloved central hawker centre in Bukit Merah, famous for its satay, char kway teow, and rojak stalls that draw regulars from all over Singapore. The satay stalls here are consistently ranked among the best in Singapore.

name: Singapore Botanic Gardens
description: Singapore's only UNESCO World Heritage Site, a 165-year-old botanical garden featuring over 10,000 species of plants, a swan lake, free-admission National Orchid Garden, and healing garden. Hosts free concerts at the Shaw Foundation Symphony Stage.

name: National Orchid Garden (Botanic Gardens)
formatted_address: 1 Cluny Road, Singapore Botanic Gardens, Singapore 259569
description: Within the Botanic Gardens, this garden holds Singapore's largest display of over 1,000 orchid species and 2,000 hybrids including the Vanda Miss Joaquim, Singapore's national flower. Celebrity orchid hybrids named after world leaders are a highlight.

name: Jacob Ballas Children's Garden (Botanic Gardens)
description: Asia's first children's garden within Singapore Botanic Gardens, designed entirely for children below 12. Features a suspension bridge, water play area, treehouse, mazes, and nature learning zones in a safe, enclosed environment.

name: Holland Village Market & Food Centre
description: A popular hawker centre in the heart of Holland Village serving great char kway teow, nasi lemak, and zi char to a diverse mix of expats, students, and locals. The buzzy alfresco area spills out onto the walkway.

name: Adam Road Food Centre
description: A heritage hawker centre near Bukit Timah known as one of the best in Singapore for Muslim hawker food including extraordinary roti john, nasi lemak, and Hainanese chicken rice. A firm favourite for both locals and expats.

name: Dempsey Hill
description: A leafy cluster of historic colonial army barracks in Singapore's central nature corridor, repurposed as a lifestyle dining and shopping village. Home to acclaimed restaurants, antique galleries, art galleries, and Cold Storage in a jungle-like setting.

name: Tanglin Mall
description: An upscale mall in the Tanglin/embassy district catering to diplomats, expats, and premium shoppers. Known for its imported groceries at Jason's supermarket, specialty boutiques, and a large collection of international cuisines.

name: Holland Road Shopping Centre
description: A cluster of shophouses and a small mall in Holland Village, famous as the go-to destination for antiques, oriental carpets, traditional Chinese artwork, and jade. An eclectic shopping experience for collectors and design lovers.

name: The Star Vista
description: A mall connected to Buona Vista MRT featuring a large performing arts theatre (The Star Theatre), food options, a supermarket, and lifestyle stores. A gateway to the one-north innovation hub and Buona Vista HDB estate.

name: Tanglin Halt Market & Food Centre
description: A venerable hawker centre in the Commonwealth estate known for its famous Tanglin Halt Original Amoy Street Food Centre rojak and outstanding goreng pisang (fried banana). One of Singapore's oldest functioning hawker centres.

name: Bishan-Ang Mo Kio Park
description: Singapore's largest park in the central region, featuring a naturalised river, dog-friendly zones, a large children's water playground, and cycling tracks. The river restoration project is a landmark achievement in biophilic urban design.

name: MacRitchie Reservoir Park
description: A 12-km trail encircling Singapore's oldest reservoir through lush rainforest, with macaques, monitor lizards, and lush vegetation. The HSBC TreeTop Walk suspension bridge offers aerial rainforest views up to 25 metres above the forest floor.

name: MacRitchie TreeTop Walk
description: A 250-metre free-standing suspension bridge linking the two highest points of MacRitchie, providing a rare bird's-eye view of the primary rainforest at 25 metres. Open only at weekends and requires a 4-km hike to reach.

name: Junction 8
description: A mid-sized community mall directly above Bishan MRT, offering a diverse range of shops, the popular Food Junction food court, a Cathay cinema, and a comprehensive mix of everyday retail and dining.

name: AMK Hub (Ang Mo Kio)
description: The largest shopping mall in the Ang Mo Kio town centre, anchored by NTUC FairPrice Finest, a Golden Village cinema complex, food court, and a wide range of everyday retail and F&B options above Ang Mo Kio MRT.

name: Thomson Plaza
description: A neighbourhood mall in Upper Thomson serving the landed property estates, with a Cold Storage supermarket, a mix of boutique restaurants, and a charming small-town atmosphere distinct from Singapore's larger malls.

name: NEX (Serangoon)
description: One of Singapore's largest suburban malls above Serangoon MRT, with a massive indoor dog run on the rooftop, a cinema, NTUC FairPrice, and over 300 stores serving the Serangoon and Hougang communities.

name: Ang Mo Kio Ave 6 Market & Food Centre
description: Popularly known as '724 AMK', this hawker centre is famed for its exceptional pan fried tofu, Lao Er Wen Chang Chicken Rice, and diverse selection of local dishes. A neighbourhood gem in the Ang Mo Kio heartlands.

name: Toa Payoh Lorong 8 Market & Food Centre
description: A busy Toa Payoh market and hawker centre famous for Tong Fong Fatt Hainanese chicken rice, laksa, and traditional Teochew kueh. Central location between Toa Payoh and Braddell MRT makes it highly accessible.

name: Jalan Kayu Prata Street
description: A famous stretch of 24-hour roti prata restaurants in the north of Singapore, dubbed the 'Prata Road'. Thasevi Food's prata is internationally acclaimed, drawing midnight roti prata lovers from all over Singapore.

name: Upper Thomson Road Food Trail
description: A scenic stretch of Upper Thomson Road lined with an eclectic mix of casual restaurants, bakeries, independent cafes, zi char stalls, and bubble tea shops. Popular with Thomson locals for leisurely weekend brunches and al fresco evening dining.

name: Hougang Mall
description: A compact neighbourhood mall above Hougang MRT serving the large northeast residential community with a NTUC FairPrice, food court, cinema, and a variety of everyday retail and F&B options.

name: Bukit Timah Nature Reserve
description: Singapore's most significant nature reserve covering 163 hectares of primary rainforest at the summit of Bukit Timah Hill (163m). Home to over 840 flowering plant species and diverse wildlife including the rare Sunda flying lemur.

name: Dairy Farm Nature Park
description: A 75-hectare nature park adjacent to Bukit Timah, featuring an old granite quarry lake, Wallace Trail, and the Wallace Education Centre honouring naturalist Alfred Russel Wallace who studied evolution here in 1854.

name: Hindhede Nature Park
description: A quiet park connecting Bukit Timah and Dairy Farm with a disused quarry trail, forest walks, and a sheltered rest area. A tranquil entry point to the Bukit Timah hill trails with ample parking.

name: Beauty World Centre
description: A Bukit Timah neighbourhood mall offering a wide range of affordable fashion, electronics, and TCM shops alongside a popular basement food court. The outdoor hawker area near Beauty World MRT is excellent for affordable local food.

name: Bukit Timah Shopping Centre
description: A neighbourhood shopping centre adjacent to Beauty World MRT with a wet market, food court, and a range of services including money changers, tailors, hair salons, and everyday retail shops.

name: Bukit Panjang Plaza
description: A community mall at the heart of Bukit Panjang town, connected to the Bukit Panjang LRT station. Serves the large northern residential community with a supermarket, a cinema, food court, and daily essentials.

name: Lot One Shoppers' Mall
description: Choa Chu Kang's main shopping destination above CCK MRT, offering a comprehensive NTUC FairPrice, Golden Village cinema, Kopitiam food court, and over 200 stores. A busy community hub for northwestern Singapore residents.

name: Singapore Sports Hub – National Stadium
description: Singapore's integrated sports complex featuring the 55,000-seat National Stadium (with the world's largest retractable dome roof), OCBC Aquatic Centre, OCBC Arena, and the Kallang Wave Mall all in one district.

name: Singapore Indoor Stadium
description: A 12,000-seat indoor arena adjacent to the National Stadium, hosting major concerts, esports events, wrestling, boxing, and other indoor sports. One of Southeast Asia's premier indoor entertainment venues.

name: Kallang Wave Mall
description: A lifestyle mall inside the Sports Hub complex specialising in sports and outdoor brands, with Decathlon, Under Armour, Adidas, and a large Sports Authority. A food court and casual dining options cater to sports fans.

name: Toa Payoh Central Food Centre
description: A well-established hawker centre in the heart of Toa Payoh estate with excellent stalls including the famous Toa Payoh Traditional Hainanese Minced Meat Noodle and a wide variety of local breakfast and lunch options.

name: Bendemeer Market & Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in Bendemeer known for its outstanding carrot cake (chai tow kway) and traditional Cantonese dim sum. A no-frills local gem popular with the Kallang-Whampoa community.

name: Whampoa Drive Makan Place
description: A beloved hawker centre in the Whampoa estate famous for Whampoa Keng Fish Head Steamboat and diverse local food stalls. One of Singapore's most atmospheric old-school hawker centres with long-established heritage stalls.

name: Punggol Waterway Park
description: A picturesque 4.2-km waterway park in the northeast, featuring water features, kayaking, cycling paths, and rest areas. The heart of Punggol's eco-town planning with lush waterside greenery and weekend activities.

name: Punggol Settlement
description: A rustic waterfront cluster of restaurants and cafes at the edge of Punggol Point, with unobstructed sea views towards Pulau Ubin. Seafood BBQ restaurants attract families for weekend outdoor dining with a laid-back kampong vibe.

name: Waterway Point
description: A large waterfront mall above Punggol MRT/LRT interchange, anchored by a NTUC FairPrice, Golden Village cinema, and a large rooftop kids' water play area. The dining and entertainment hub for Singapore's newest eco-town.

name: Sengkang Riverside Park
description: A lush riverside park along Sungei Punggol waterway with a boardwalk, fishing areas, jogging track, and a weekend farmers' market. Popular with the Sengkang-Punggol community for outdoor recreation.

name: Coney Island (Pulau Serangoon)
description: A rustic island nature park accessible by foot or bicycle from Punggol, featuring secondary forest, coastal grasslands, wild otters, and mangroves. A rare escapist destination that still feels untouched by urban development.

name: Lorong Halus Wetland
description: A unique wetland park built over a former landfill site, with boardwalk trails through marshland, bird hides, and one of the best birdwatching spots in Singapore. Spot herons, egrets, and migratory birds on the waterway.

name: Kranji War Memorial
description: Singapore's most important war memorial, honouring 24,000 Allied soldiers who died in the WWII Malayan Campaign and Battle of Singapore. Features the State Cemetery, 11 Christian and multi-faith plots, and immaculate war graves maintained by the Commonwealth War Graves Commission.

name: Sun Yat Sen Nanyang Memorial Hall
description: A colonial black-and-white bungalow that served as Dr Sun Yat-sen's temporary headquarters during his fundraising trips to Singapore, now a museum tracing his revolutionary activities and the Nanyang Chinese community's role in the Chinese Revolution of 1911.

name: Singapore City Gallery
description: An exhibition hall in the URA Centre showcasing Singapore's extraordinary urban planning story through an enormous 3D city scale model and interactive exhibits. Essential for understanding how Singapore transformed from fishing village to global city.

name: Former Ford Factory (Reflections at Bukit Chandu)
description: The historic building where Lieutenant-General Percival surrendered to General Yamashita on 15 February 1942, marking the fall of Singapore to Japan. Now a WWII museum with multimedia exhibits, photographs, and oral histories of the Japanese Occupation.

name: Lian Shan Shuang Lin Temple
description: Singapore's largest Buddhist temple complex and a National Monument, first built in 1898. The three-storey pagoda, drum tower, and beautifully carved stone columns in the main hall represent the finest of southern Chinese temple architecture in Southeast Asia.

name: Hong San See Temple
description: A National Monument Hokkien temple dedicated to Guan Di (the God of War and Brotherhood) built in 1908. Distinguished by its exquisite Fujian craftwork of ceramic figurines, hand-painted murals, and intricately carved roof ridges.

name: Tan Si Chong Su Temple
description: A National Monument clan house temple founded by the Tan clan (a Hokkien surname association) in 1878, featuring classical south Fujian architecture with glazed tile roofs, granite columns, and elaborate woodcarvings overlooking the Singapore River.

name: Masjid Omar Kampong Melaka
description: Singapore's second oldest mosque, built in 1820, initially serving the Malay Bugis and Indonesian immigrant community. Its modest Malay architecture with a tiered roof is a National Monument in the Clarke Quay-Merchant Road heritage precinct.

name: Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve
description: Singapore's premier nature reserve and an ASEAN Heritage Park, protecting over 130 hectares of pristine mangroves, mudflats, and secondary forest. Spot migratory shorebirds, saltwater crocodiles, monitor lizards, and over 140 bird species.

name: Pulau Ubin
description: A rustic island off northeastern Singapore where time seems to have stood still. Reachable by bumboat from Changi Point Ferry Terminal, Pulau Ubin offers mountain biking, mangrove walks, Chek Jawa wetlands, and old kampong houses.

name: Chek Jawa Wetlands (Pulau Ubin)
description: One of Singapore's last intact coastal ecosystems at the eastern tip of Pulau Ubin, featuring six ecosystems (rocky beach, sea grass lagoon, coral rubble beach, scrubland, coastal hills, and mangroves) on a 1-km boardwalk.

name: Singapore Maritime Gallery
description: A free-admission maritime museum at HarbourFront showcasing Singapore's evolution as one of the world's busiest ports. Interactive exhibits cover maritime history, shipping technology, and Singapore's role as a global trade hub.

name: Keramat Iskandar Shah (Fort Canning)
description: A sacred Malay royal shrine on Fort Canning Hill marking the burial site of Iskandar Shah, the last ruler of ancient Singapura in the 14th century. One of the oldest heritage sites in Singapore, predating British colonial rule by five centuries.

name: Nagore Dargah Indian Muslim Heritage Centre
description: Singapore's first museum dedicated to the history and culture of Indian Muslims (Chulias), sharing stories of early traders, merchants, and labourers who shaped Singapore's diverse religious landscape. Housed in a restored 1820s shrine.

name: Redhill Market & Food Centre
description: A popular Bukit Merah hawker centre famous for its good value economic rice (cai fan), fried carrot cake, and wanton mee. Well-loved by the surrounding HDB community for consistent quality and affordable meals.

name: Queenstown Market & Food Centre
description: A comprehensive wet market and hawker centre in Queenstown, one of Singapore's oldest housing estates. Known for quality fishmongers downstairs and good variety of hawker food including nasi lemak and mee siam upstairs.

name: Commonwealth Crescent Market & Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in the Queenstown-Commonwealth area popular with locals for its legendary char kway teow, nasi padang, and the famous Commonwealth Crescent Yu Kee Duck Rice stall.

name: Margaret Drive Hawker Centre
description: A quiet hawker centre near Queenstown MRT known among foodies for its exceptional pig organ soup, satay beehoon, and tze char (cook-to-order) dishes. A true neighbourhood gem that rewards those who seek it out.

name: Mei Chin Road Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in the Queensway area beloved for its excellent popiah, carrot cake, and Indian rojak. Serves the Dawson and Mei Chin estate communities with a loyal following of regulars.

name: Telok Blangah Drive Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in Telok Blangah serving the residential community with a variety of Chinese, Malay, and Indian hawker dishes. Close to HarbourFront and the Southern Ridges walking trail.

name: Pek Kio Market & Food Centre
description: A well-regarded hawker centre near Novena, known for its excellent Hainanese chicken rice and variety of noodle dishes. Popular with the Novena-Toa Payoh community for consistent quality and affordable pricing.

name: Bukit Merah Central Food Centre
description: A lively hawker centre in Bukit Merah serving a diverse mix of Chinese, Indian, and Malay cuisine. Known for its outstanding economy rice and a wide selection of traditional kueh. A reliable neighbourhood food hub.

name: Geylang Bahru Market & Food Centre
description: A conveniently located hawker centre between Boon Keng and Geylang MRT, popular for its seafood bee hoon, carrot cake, and morning dim sum. A neighbourhood staple for Geylang Bahru residents.

name: Boon Keng Market & Food Centre
description: A small but well-stocked hawker centre and wet market above Boon Keng MRT, serving the surrounding residential community with fresh produce, Chinese provisions, and a selection of local hawker dishes.

name: Yishun Ring Road Hawker Centre
description: A busy neighbourhood hawker centre in Yishun known for its excellent popiah, nasi lemak, and local desserts. Serves the large Yishun residential population as one of the key food hubs in Singapore's north.

name: Sembawang Hills Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in Sembawang Hills serving local food classics including claypot rice, wonton mee, and a popular Teochew porridge stall. Well-known within the Sembawang community for consistently good food.

name: Marsiling Lane Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in the Marsiling estate offering diverse local dishes at very affordable prices. Well-loved by Woodlands and Marsiling residents for its BBQ chicken wings, fish soup, and local desserts.

name: Taman Jurong Market & Food Centre
description: A comprehensive market and hawker centre in Taman Jurong serving the west Singapore community with a wet market, hawker food, and a wide range of Chinese, Malay, and Indian dishes. Excellent for morning fish and vegetable shopping.

name: Boon Lay Place Market & Food Village
description: A large hawker centre and wet market in Boon Lay serving the west Singapore community, popular for its fish ball noodles, nasi padang, and char siew (BBQ pork) rice. One of the go-to food centres for western Singapore residents.

name: Jurong West Street 52 Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in Jurong West serving one of Singapore's largest HDB estates. Known for its outstanding hor fun (flat rice noodles in gravy) and economical set meals popular with families in the west.

name: Velocity@Novena Square
description: Singapore's dedicated sports lifestyle mall adjacent to Novena MRT, anchored by Decathlon, Royal Sporting House, and fitness studios. The mall connects to Square 2 and the Tan Tock Seng Hospital medical cluster.

name: Square 2 (Novena)
description: A lifestyle mall adjacent to Velocity@Novena Square and Novena MRT, featuring health and wellness brands, medical specialist clinics, F&B outlets, and a Cold Storage supermarket. Convenient for healthcare and everyday shopping.

name: United Square (Novena)
description: Singapore's premier children's and family mall near Novena, with three floors dedicated to children's enrichment centres, baby products, family dining, and educational toys. Beloved by parents in the central area.

name: Funan
description: A revamped City Hall mall with a focus on technology, active lifestyle, and creative culture. Features a rooftop urban farm, indoor climbing wall, cycling path through the mall, and curated tech and sporting goods retailers.

name: Capitol Piazza
description: A premium mixed-use development integrating the restored 1929 Capitol Theatre into a boutique retail and hotel complex. Features gourmet F&B, the Patina Capitol hotel, and luxury apartments in the heart of the civic district.

name: Sim Lim Tower
description: A six-storey electronics and electrical goods mall at the junction of Jalan Besar and Rochor Road, distinct from (and older than) the nearby Sim Lim Square. Specialises in electrical components, audio equipment, and second-hand electronics.

name: Rivervale Mall
description: A neighbourhood mall in Sengkang serving the Rivervale and Fernvale communities with a NTUC FairPrice, food court, and everyday retail. A convenient community hub connected to the Sengkang LRT network.

name: Punggol 21 Hawker Centre
description: A modern hawker centre in Singapore's newest eco-town of Punggol featuring spacious ventilated units, diverse food options from nasi lemak to Japanese ramen, and a family-friendly design with ample seating.

name: Eastpoint Mall (Simei)
description: A neighbourhood mall in Simei above Simei MRT on the East West Line, with a NTUC FairPrice, food court, and a variety of family-oriented retail and F&B outlets for eastern Singapore residents.

name: Tampines 1
description: The trendiest of Tampines' three malls, featuring youth-focused fashion, beauty, and lifestyle brands, a cinema, a food court, and dining street. Part of the Tampines Regional Centre shopping cluster around Tampines MRT.

name: White Sands (Pasir Ris)
description: The main shopping hub for the Pasir Ris town, offering a comprehensive mix of retail, food court, cinema, Fairprice Finest supermarket, and a family-friendly environment. Above Pasir Ris MRT on the East West Line.

name: Elias Mall
description: A compact neighbourhood mall in the Elias estate near Pasir Ris Park, offering a NTUC FairPrice, a food court with local hawker fare, and essential services for the surrounding HDB community.

name: WestMall (Bukit Batok)
description: Bukit Batok's main shopping mall above Bukit Batok MRT, featuring a NTUC FairPrice, Shaw Theatres cinema, food court, and everyday retail stores serving the large Bukit Batok residential community.

name: Sisters' Islands Marine Park
description: Singapore's first marine park consisting of Big Sister's Island and Little Sister's Island, known for excellent snorkelling in coral-rich waters, sandy beaches, and camping. Accessible by private boat or organised tours from Marina South Pier.

name: Bukit Batok Nature Park
description: A lush 36-hectare park with an impressive former quarry lake (Little Guilin), granite cliffs, and forested walking trails connecting to the Bukit Timah Nature Reserve. Known as 'Little Guilin' for its resemblance to the scenic Chinese city.

name: Admiralty Park
description: Singapore's largest children's playground area park in the north, featuring an 880-metre-long outdoor obstacle course through the forest, and hundreds of individual play elements making it a fantastic family destination.

name: Sembawang Hot Spring Park
description: Singapore's only natural hot spring, featuring outdoor foot bathing pools with naturally heated groundwater at about 70°C, a heritage hut, and landscaped gardens. A unique and relaxing wellness experience in northern Singapore.

name: one-north Park
description: A 17-hectare green spine running through the one-north research and innovation hub, with themed gardens representing different scientific disciplines, sky bridges, and art installations. A tranquil green corridor for tech workers and families.

name: Marina Bay Golf Course
description: A public 18-hole golf course on Marina East with a stunning city skyline backdrop across the reservoir. Offers affordable public golf in an unbeatable location between Tanah Merah and Marina Bay, with floodlit evening play available.

name: Palawan Beach – Southernmost Point of Continental Asia
description: A small island accessible by a swaying footbridge from Palawan Beach, marking the southernmost point of continental Asia. A popular milestone for travellers, with GPS coordinates and a photo opportunity at the edge of the Asian continent.

name: The Promontory @ Marina Bay
description: A public waterfront promenade on the Marina Bay shoreline with unobstructed views of Gardens by the Bay, Marina Bay Sands, and the Helix Bridge. A quieter alternative to Merlion Park for scenic waterfront photography.

name: Tiong Bahru Bakery (Original Outlet)
description: The original Tiong Bahru Bakery renowned for its buttery, flaky croissants and artisan French pastries, located in the iconic art deco Tiong Bahru estate. A must-visit for morning coffee and breakfast in one of Singapore's trendiest neighbourhoods.

name: Atlas Bar
formatted_address: 600 North Bridge Road, Parkview Square, Singapore 188778
description: An award-winning Art Deco bar inside the iconic Parkview Square ('Gotham Building'), housing the world's largest collection of gin (over 1,200 bottles) in a four-storey gilded lobby. Consistently ranked among Asia's 50 Best Bars.

name: Raffles Hotel Long Bar
description: The birthplace of the Singapore Sling cocktail (invented in 1915 by bartender Ngiam Tong Boon), the Long Bar at Raffles Hotel is an iconic colonial-era watering hole where guests traditionally throw peanut shells on the floor.

name: llaollao (ION Orchard)
description: A popular Spanish frozen yoghurt chain known for its signature Sanum (large tub) of creamy froyo with colourful toppings. The ION Orchard basement outlet draws long queues from shoppers seeking a refreshing cold treat.

name: Song Fa Bak Kut Teh (New Bridge Road)
description: A Michelin Bib Gourmand bak kut teh (pork rib soup) institution with a 50-year history, famous for its clear Teochew-style peppery broth and tender fall-off-the-bone pork ribs. Always has a queue but moves fast.

name: Ya Kun Kaya Toast (Far East Plaza)
formatted_address: 14 Scotts Road, #01-24 Far East Plaza, Singapore 228213
description: The Far East Plaza outlet of Singapore's most iconic kaya toast chain, serving traditional Singapore breakfasts of kaya-butter toast, soft-boiled eggs, and kopi (traditional coffee) since 1944. An essential Singapore culinary experience.

name: Jumbo Seafood (East Coast Seafood Centre)
description: The East Coast Park outlet of Singapore's most famous chilli crab restaurant, offering the definitive version of Singapore's beloved national dish alongside black pepper crab, cereal prawns, and steamed fish with a sea breeze backdrop.

name: Siglap Market & Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in the Siglap estate known for its excellent economic rice, prawn paste chicken wings, and strong coffee. A reliable local food hub for the upper east coast residential community.

name: Tampines Round Market & Food Centre
description: Tampines' most popular wet market and hawker centre, famous for its circular architecture and outstanding selection of fresh produce. The Hua Yu Wee Seafood Restaurant at the adjacent waterfront is excellent for crab.

name: Upper Changi Road North Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre serving the Upper Changi and Loyang estates with a comprehensive selection of local dishes including Malay nasi lemak, Chinese fishball noodles, and Indian roti prata.

name: Bedok North Avenue 1 Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in Bedok North known for its outstanding prawn mee (Hokkien prawn noodle soup) and economical meal options. Popular with Bedok North residents as an everyday dining destination.

name: Katong Laksa (East Coast Road Row)
description: The world-famous Katong laksa district where multiple competing stalls claim to serve the original spicy coconut milk laksa with thick short noodles and cockles. The best laksa in Singapore debate has raged here for decades.

name: Esplanade – Outdoor Theatre
description: The free outdoor performance space adjacent to the Esplanade building, hosting free live music, dance, and theatre performances under the open sky facing Marina Bay. Check the Esplanade Free Series programme for weekly free shows.

name: Singapore Discovery Centre
description: An experiential museum exploring Singapore's total defence concept, national security, and future technologies through interactive exhibits and immersive experiences. Features a 4D theatre and outdoor shooting range near the western forests.

name: Snow City Singapore
description: Singapore's only permanent indoor snow centre adjacent to Science Centre, maintaining a year-round temperature of -5°C with a 60-metre snowslide, snowplay areas, and winter exhibits. A unique tropical weather contrast experience.

name: SNG Moh Road Cooked Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in the Tanglin-Holland fringe popular for its excellent laksa, fried carrot cake, and yong tau foo. A well-regarded community food centre for the Tanglin-Holland residential community.

name: Labrador Park Coastal Walk
description: A scenic coastal walking path along the rocky shores of Labrador Nature Reserve, with views of the Strait of Singapore and anchored oil tankers. Includes a historic WWII gun battery trail, coastal cliffs, and rocky tidal pools.

name: Pulau Ubin – Cycling Trails
description: A network of dirt and trail cycling routes across Pulau Ubin's 10 sq km, passing abandoned quarry lakes, kampong houses, Buddhist temples, and mangrove forests. Bikes are rented cheaply at the main village jetty.

name: Bukit Batok – Little Guilin (Quarry Lake)
description: A photogenic disused quarry lake in Bukit Batok Nature Park with granite cliffs reflected in the still water, resembling the famous karst landscape of Guilin, China. Popular for jogging, photography, and peaceful morning walks.

name: Pasir Ris Park – Mangrove Boardwalk
formatted_address: Pasir Ris Park, Pasir Ris Drive 1, Singapore 519147
description: A 6-hectare mangrove forest within Pasir Ris Park featuring a raised boardwalk trail through the tidal forest ecosystem. Spot mudskippers, fiddler crabs, mangrove kingfishers, and rare tree species rarely seen in urban Singapore.

name: Chinese Heritage Centre (NTU)
description: A museum within Nanyang Technological University's heritage campus tracing the story of overseas Chinese in Southeast Asia. Housed in a magnificent 1920s southern Chinese architecture building, originally Nanyang University's administration block.

name: Old Jurong Bird Park (Site) – Now Bird Paradise
description: The site of the former Jurong Bird Park (1971-2022), now being redeveloped. Jurong Hill adjacent park offers the best remaining city views from the Jurong hilltop and a peaceful secondary forest walk for the western community.

name: Singapore Turf Club (Kranji)
description: Singapore's only horse racing venue, hosting approximately 50 race meetings per year at Kranji Racecourse. Non-race days are open for track workouts and tours. The annual Singapore Derby and Queen Elizabeth II Cup attract large crowds.

name: Seletar Aerospace Park – Heritage Trail
description: A heritage walk through Seletar Aerospace Park's colonial bungalows and pre-war RAF Seletar airbase buildings, now revitalised into restaurants, galleries, and creative studios within a lush black-and-white bungalow enclave.

name: Upper Seletar Reservoir Park
