description: A large reservoir park north of Mandai with a tranquil waterside trail through secondary forest, fishing jetties, and a recreational park. A quieter alternative to MacRitchie and Bishan-AMK Park.

name: Central Catchment Nature Reserve
formatted_address: Singapore Central Water Catchment Area, Singapore
description: The largest nature reserve in Singapore covering 2,800 hectares of primary and secondary rainforest around the four central reservoirs. Home to 73 mammal species, 500+ plant species, and the critically endangered Singapore banded leaf monkey.

name: Malay Heritage Centre (Istana Kampong Glam)
description: Singapore's national museum dedicated to Malay history and culture, housed in the restored Istana Kampong Glam (the former royal palace). Features exhibits on traditional Malay life, the royal family, and the Malay-Islamic trade world of the 19th century.

name: ArtScience Museum – Future World (Permanent)
description: The permanent Future World gallery within ArtScience Museum by teamLab, featuring 16 immersive interactive digital art installations including the Crystal Universe, Living Digital Forest, and a room of floating orbs that respond to touch.

name: OCBC Aquatic Centre (Sports Hub)
description: Singapore's national aquatic centre with Olympic-standard 50-metre competition pool, leisure pools, and water slides. Open to the public for lap swimming, learn-to-swim programs, and casual water play. Part of the Singapore Sports Hub.

name: Singapore River Heritage Walk
description: A 3-km heritage promenade along both banks of the Singapore River from Robertson Quay to Merlion Park, passing Boat Quay, Clarke Quay, and historic landmarks. Sculptures, plaques, and restored godowns tell Singapore's trading history.

name: Kopitiam Food Court (VivoCity)
description: A large food court on Level 3 of VivoCity with a comprehensive selection of local hawker favourites from chicken rice to wonton mee, offering affordable meals adjacent to HarbourFront's premium dining options.

name: Geylang Lor 9 – Fresh Frog Porridge
description: A famous late-night outdoor food cluster in Geylang specialising in fresh frog leg porridge, a beloved Singaporean delicacy. Best eaten after 10 PM when the steaming clay pots of frog congee draw long queues of foodies.

name: Roland Restaurant
description: A legendary Singaporean seafood restaurant established in 1956, credited with helping popularise chilli crab nationwide. The Marine Parade location has served three generations of Singaporeans and remains one of the city's most beloved institutions.

name: Long Beach Seafood (Dempsey)
description: An upscale seafood restaurant at Dempsey Hill known for its signature Black Pepper Crab (a dish they claim to have invented in the 1980s) and live seafood tanks. The lush jungle setting in the colonial Dempsey barracks adds ambience.

name: Mellben Seafood (Ang Mo Kio)
description: A popular AMK seafood restaurant renowned for its outstanding claypot crab beehoon (rice vermicelli with crab in rich seafood broth) and consistently excellent zi char dishes. Always packed with families for dinner.

name: No Signboard Seafood (Esplanade)
description: Famous for its white pepper crab, No Signboard Seafood at Esplanade Mall combines a prime Marina Bay location with one of Singapore's most celebrated crab recipes. The white pepper sauce is their signature invention.

name: Pince & Pints (Duxton Hill)
description: A celebrated Duxton Road lobster roll restaurant pairing generous portions of butter-soaked Maine lobster with craft beer. The Portland (cold) and Connecticut (hot) rolls are among Singapore's most coveted weekend dining experiences.

name: Gluttons Bay (Esplanade)
description: A curated open-air hawker area hand-picked by the Makansutra food guide, facing Marina Bay from the Esplanade promenade. Serves top hawker classics like oyster omelette, fried carrot cake, and BBQ sambal stingray.

name: Swee Choon Tim Sum (Jalan Besar)
description: Singapore's most famous late-night dim sum institution, open until dawn and serving the city's best har gow, siew mai, and char siew bao to families, clubbers, and night-shift workers since 1962. A Jalan Besar tradition for 60 years.

name: Imperial Treasure Nan Bei Restaurant (Orchard)
formatted_address: 391 Orchard Road, #04-01 Ngee Ann City, Singapore 238872
description: A fine Chinese dining restaurant at Ngee Ann City offering exceptional Northern and Cantonese cuisine. The Peking Duck (requiring 24-hour advance order) and Boston Lobster noodles are signature dishes beloved by discerning diners.

name: Crystal Jade La Mian Xiao Long Bao (Orchard)
description: A beloved Singapore restaurant chain serving handmade la mian (hand-pulled noodles), xiao long bao (soup dumplings), and Shanghai dim sum. The ION Orchard outlet is consistently popular for its quality and value.

name: Paradise Dynasty (ION Orchard)
description: Famous for its 8-colour xiao long bao featuring different flavoured skins (original, foie gras, black truffle, etc.) and exceptional Shanghainese cuisine. A major drawcard for tourists and food lovers at ION Orchard.

name: Tian Tian Hainanese Chicken Rice (Maxwell)
formatted_address: 1 Kadayanallur Street, #01-10 Maxwell Food Centre, Singapore 069184
description: Singapore's most famous chicken rice stall, awarded a Michelin Bib Gourmand. The queue starts forming before opening; Anthony Bourdain famously called this Singapore's best chicken rice. Sells out daily, typically by 3 PM.

name: Nam Kee Pau (Upper Serangoon Road)
description: A 50-year-old Serangoon Gardens bakery institution renowned for its handmade char siew pau (BBQ pork buns) and traditional steamed pau varieties. The soft, fluffy dough and generous filling are the gold standard for Singapore pau.

name: Whampoa Keng Fish Head Steamboat
description: A heritage seafood restaurant famed for its unique Teochew-style fish head steamboat served in a piping hot claypot broth at your table. A beloved Singapore institution that has served the same family recipe for over 50 years.

name: Springleaf Prata Place
description: A famous 24-hour prata restaurant in the Yishun-Upper Thomson area known for creative prata innovations including mushroom cheese prata, banana chocolate prata, and the classic kosong (plain). A north Singapore institution.

name: Punggol Nasi Lemak (Ponggol) – Mattar Road
description: Singapore's most famous nasi lemak (coconut rice) stall, operating since 1973 in various iterations. The pandan-scented coconut rice with crispy fried chicken, sambal, and ikan bilis draws hour-long queues every night.

name: Sprout Hub (SkyVille@Dawson)
description: Singapore's largest indoor urban farming hub inside a HDB estate, featuring rooftop farms, hydroponics displays, a farm-to-table café, and agricultural education programmes. A unique demonstration of Singapore's food resilience agenda.

name: Suntec City – North and East Wings
description: The expanded north and east wings of Suntec City mall with a refreshed retail lineup including more F&B, lifestyle stores, and a covered connection to the Singapore International Convention & Exhibition Centre (SUNTEC Singapore).

name: Cathay – The Cathay (Dhoby Ghaut)
description: A mixed-use complex at Dhoby Ghaut featuring the Cathay Cineplexes, a heritage gallery about the Cathay family's entertainment history, casual dining, and a lifestyle mall with youth-oriented retail above Dhoby Ghaut MRT.

name: Palais Renaissance
description: A boutique luxury mall on upper Orchard Road housing premium lifestyle tenants including Porsche Design, Clarins, and high-end beauty brands, plus the Michelin-starred Restaurant Zen and fine dining options.

name: Orchard Gateway
description: Two glass-and-steel towers connected by a distinctive glass sky bridge spanning Orchard Road, housing TANGS Studio, restaurants, cafes, and a roof garden. A striking architectural addition to the Orchard Road skyline.

name: DFS Galleria Singapore (Scotts Walk)
description: Singapore's premier duty-free luxury shopping experience for international travellers, offering beauty, fashion, spirits, and jewellery from top luxury brands at duty-free prices. Exclusively for international passport holders.

name: TANGS (Orchard)
description: Singapore's homegrown iconic department store at the corner of Orchard and Scotts Roads, established in 1958. TANGS is known for its distinctive pagoda-inspired roof, curated selection of local and international brands, and TANGS Studio.

name: Orchard Road – Full Shopping Belt Walk
description: A 2.2 km stretch of world-class shopping, dining, and entertainment along Singapore's most famous boulevard. With 23 malls, over 5,000 hotel rooms, and iconic Christmas decorations every December, Orchard Road is Singapore's heartbeat.

name: Jewel Changi Airport
description: A spectacular glass-and-steel dome housing the world's tallest indoor waterfall (Rain Vortex at 40m) surrounded by the world's largest indoor garden (Shiseido Forest Valley). Features over 280 shops, restaurants, and attractions.

name: Jewel Changi – Rain Vortex & Shiseido Forest Valley
formatted_address: 78 Airport Boulevard, #01-701 Jewel Changi Airport, Singapore 819666
description: The centrepiece of Jewel Changi Airport — the Rain Vortex, the world's tallest indoor waterfall cascading 40 metres from the glass oculus roof, surrounded by a lush 4-storey terraced forest garden with over 3,000 plant species.

name: Canopy Park (Jewel Changi Airport)
formatted_address: 78 Airport Boulevard, L5 Jewel Changi Airport, Singapore 819666
description: A rooftop park on Level 5 of Jewel Changi featuring a bouncing net (Bouncing Net), walking net (Walking Net), hedge maze, mirror maze, topiaries, and Singapore's first indoor sky net. A playful aerial experience above the Rain Vortex.

name: Changi Airport – Terminal 3 Attractions
description: Beyond aviation, Changi Airport Terminal 3 offers a free Cinema, cactus garden, butterfly garden, and 24-hour retail and dining even for non-travellers. The famous Changi Airport has won 'World's Best Airport' a record 12 times.

name: Changi Airport – Terminal 2 Sunflower Garden
description: Terminal 2's Sunflower Garden is a massive indoor garden featuring a 3-storey vine-covered structure with a stunning art installation, retail therapy, and dining options. Free for public visitors even without a flight.

name: Fuk Tak Chi Museum (Far East Square)
description: Singapore's smallest museum housed in an 1824 Hakka-Cantonese temple, dedicated to the God of Prosperity. The two-room museum displays artefacts, photos, and documents from the early Hokka immigrant community era.

name: NUS Baba House (Neil Road)
description: A meticulously restored 1895 Peranakan townhouse at Neil Road managed by NUS, open for weekend guided tours only. The authentically furnished interiors of a prosperous Baba family home are one of Singapore's finest Peranakan experiences.

name: Keong Saik Bakery
description: A beloved neighbourhood artisan bakery on Keong Saik Road, known for its outstanding sourdough, croissants, and local-inspired pastries like kaya toast croissant and pandan coconut tart. Popular for weekend breakfast queues.

name: Hwa Chong Mansion Heritage Collection
description: A heritage museum within the grounds of Hwa Chong Institution tracing the history of the iconic 1920s colonial schoolhouse and Singapore's Chinese middle school education movement. Features rare photographs and academic artefacts.

name: Tiong Bahru Community Centre (Social Space)
description: The community centre at the heart of Tiong Bahru estate, hosting fitness classes, art workshops, cultural programmes, and the famous monthly antique market. A great way to experience neighbourhood life in Singapore's trendiest old estate.

name: Geylang East Home / Lions Befrienders (Community)
description: The Geylang East industrial and community area adjacent to the Kolam Ayer estate, with a cluster of clan associations, Chinese opera theatres, and neighbourhood shops. An authentic slice of old working-class Singapore.

name: Jurong Hill (Former Jurong Park)
description: A 65-metre elevated hilltop park offering the best panoramic view of the Jurong Industrial Estate and the Port of Singapore. A short forested walk leads to the hilltop observation deck.

name: Seletar Country Club Dining
description: The heritage Seletar Country Club set amid colonial bungalows and lush greenery, with restaurant and sports facilities open to non-members for dining. The Seletar Aerospace area nearby has excellent casual dining spots in restored RAF bungalows.

name: Bottle Tree Park (Sembawang)
description: A rustic outdoor dining and recreational park near Sembawang with seafood restaurants, kampong-style chalets, fishing ponds, and bird aviaries. A favourite family destination for outdoor recreation and fresh seafood in the north.

name: Tang Tea House (Chinatown)
description: A traditional Teochew dim sum restaurant in the heart of Chinatown serving classic Hong Kong-style dim sum in a no-frills but reliable setting. Popular for consistent quality dim sum throughout the day, not just at lunchtime.

name: China Square Food Centre
description: A popular weekday-only hawker centre in the Telok Ayer CBD area serving the lunchtime office crowd with excellent economy rice, roasted meats, and noodle soups. Calm and peaceful on weekends when offices close.

name: Lim Chee Guan Bak Kwa (Chinatown)
description: Singapore's most celebrated bak kwa (barbecued pork jerky) shop, with queues wrapping around the block especially before Chinese New Year. The thin-sliced, charcoal-grilled sweet-savoury pork is Singapore's most beloved festive snack.

name: Bengawan Solo (The Centrepoint)
description: Singapore's most beloved Peranakan kueh shop, founded in 1979. Known for their signature pandan layer cake and pillowy soft pandan chiffon cake, Bengawan Solo is synonymous with Nonya pastries and Singapore's culinary heritage.

name: Ghim Moh Road Market & Food Centre
description: A combined market and hawker centre near Buona Vista MRT famed for its outstanding onde-onde, kueh dadar (pandan coconut roll), and Nonya desserts. A Peranakan culinary hub loved by locals and kueh enthusiasts.

name: Tiong Bahru Mian Jia (Tiong Bahru Market)
formatted_address: 30 Seng Poh Road, #02-26 Tiong Bahru Market, Singapore 168898
description: The most famous handmade wonton noodle stall in Tiong Bahru Market, where Mr Tan has been hand-rolling egg noodles daily since the 1970s. The springy noodles and plump wontons are considered by many to be Singapore's best.

name: Marine Parade Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre serving the Marine Parade community with a wide variety of local dishes. Famous for its excellent wonton mee stalls and nearby to the East Coast Road Peranakan food trail.

name: SPRMRKT Café (Robertson Walk)
description: A popular Robertson Quay lifestyle café and brunch spot combining specialty coffee, artisanal pastries, and a gourmet grocery-retail concept. Well-known among Singapore's café culture aficionados for weekend brunch.

name: Neon Pigeon (Tanjong Pagar)
description: A creative izakaya-inspired restaurant in Tanjong Pagar blending Japanese ingredients with local Singaporean flavours in a casual, buzzy setting. Popular for creative cocktails and innovative small plates for sharing.

name: Kallang Riverside Park
description: A waterway park along the Kallang Basin and Geylang River offering scenic jogging paths, kayaking access points, and views of the Singapore Sports Hub. A pleasant outdoor recreation area connecting to East Coast Park via the coastal trail.

name: Bedok Reservoir Park
description: A scenic 88-hectare reservoir park in the east offering a 4.5 km lakeside jogging loop, kayaking, and water skiing. The tranquil morning atmosphere with anglers, joggers, and cyclists makes it a beloved east Singapore escape.

name: Punggol Beach & Coastal Walk
description: A scenic coastal walk along Punggol Point with views of Pulau Ubin, Johor, and the shipping lanes. Bumboat launches to Pulau Ubin depart from Changi Point, while the beach walk offers a quiet nature retreat in Singapore's newest town.

name: East Coast Park – Cycling & Beach Recreation
formatted_address: East Coast Park, East Coast Parkway, Singapore 449875
description: A dedicated 15 km coastal cycling park where bicycles and rollerblades can be rented hourly. Popular on weekends with families, featuring beach volleyball courts, casual dining kiosks, and barbecue pits along the way.

name: Gallop Stable (Pasir Ris)
description: A full-service equestrian facility in Pasir Ris offering riding lessons for beginners and advanced riders, pony rides for children, and holiday horse riding camps. One of Singapore's few remaining outdoor riding schools.

name: Singapore Wake Park (Punggol)
description: Singapore's premier cable wake park on Punggol Reservoir, offering wakeboarding and waterskiing for all skill levels without a boat. Features a cable system that pulls riders across the water through fun obstacles and ramps.

name: New Asia Bar (Swissôtel The Stamford)
description: A dramatic sky bar perched on the 71st floor of Swissôtel The Stamford offering arguably Singapore's best 360-degree city view. The bar combines creative cocktails with panoramic vistas of Marina Bay and the Strait of Singapore.

name: 1-Altitude Gallery & Bar
description: A world-famous open-air rooftop bar on the 63rd floor of One Raffles Place, the highest rooftop bar in Singapore. Combines breathtaking 360-degree city views with creative cocktails and bar bites in the cool evening breeze.

name: Level 33 Craft Brewery (Marina Bay)
description: The world's highest urban craft brewery, brewing its own ales, lagers, and stouts on Level 33 of Marina Bay Financial Centre Tower 1. The panoramic views of Marina Bay from the brewing tanks are spectacular.

name: Geylang Serai Ramadan Bazaar (Annual)
description: A spectacular annual night bazaar held at Geylang Serai during the Ramadan month, transforming the area into a giant food and cultural market. Thousands of stalls sell Malay delicacies, kueh, and festive goods under colourful lights.

name: Tekka Centre Hawker (Zhu Jiao) – Upper Floor
description: The bustling upper floor of the Tekka Centre hawker centre, offering a broad range of Indian, Malay, and Chinese food at affordable prices. The mee goreng, nasi briyani, and thosai are exceptional here.

name: Ang Mo Kio Ave 1 Market (511 AMK)
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in the heart of AMK, famous for its outstanding cha soba (Japanese buckwheat noodles), wonton mee, and diverse breakfast options. One of AMK's most active community food hubs.

name: HDB Hub (Toa Payoh) – Food Court
description: A busy food court in the basement of the HDB Hub (Housing Development Board headquarters) at Toa Payoh, serving civil servants and the public with affordable local favourites including chicken rice, mixed vegetable rice, and local desserts.

name: Ayer Rajah Food Centre
description: A popular hawker centre in the west near one-north, beloved for its exceptional curry fish head, BBQ satay, and claypot chicken rice. Attracts workers from the adjacent Science Parks and one-north tech hub.

name: Coronation Plaza Food Court
description: A small food court in Coronation Plaza near the Botanic Gardens serving the landed property community of the Holland-Bukit Timah corridor, with excellent nasi lemak, chicken rice, and local Indian food.

name: Serangoon Garden Way Eateries
description: The charming main street of Serangoon Gardens estate with a cluster of family-friendly restaurants, ice cream shops, and cafes. The area is particularly well known for its hawker fare at Chomp Chomp and a range of Western casual dining options.

name: Food Junction (United Square)
description: A family-oriented food court at United Square in Novena, offering a diverse selection of hawker-style dishes particularly popular at lunchtime with parents and children from the nearby enrichment centres and schools.

name: Hougang Avenue 6 Hawker Centre
description: A bustling northeast hawker centre in Hougang New Town known for its diverse food offerings and affordable pricing. Popular with Hougang and Kovan residents for everyday meals from morning kopi to dinner zi char.

name: Sims Vista Market & Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre and wet market near Aljunied MRT serving the diverse Geylang-Aljunied community with Chinese, Malay, and Indian food. Known for good BBQ meats and morning dim sum.

name: Punggol Plaza Food Court
description: A neighbourhood food court in Punggol Plaza serving the Punggol residential community with a wide range of local dishes. Convenient for residents of the Punggol Waterway and Northshore districts.

name: Tampines Street 11 Hawker Centre
description: One of Tampines' beloved neighbourhood hawker centres, serving the large Tampines HDB estate with diverse local food. Known for excellent fish soup, fried Hokkien mee, and Indian economy rice.

name: Woodlands Street 12 Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in Woodlands serving the large residential community near Woodlands MRT with everyday local fare including nasi lemak, chicken rice, and wonton mee at affordable prices.

name: Clementi Avenue 2 Market & Food Centre
description: A comprehensive hawker centre and wet market in Clementi known for excellent Malay nasi lemak, prawn paste fried rice, and a wide range of morning breakfast options popular with NUS students and families.

name: The Intan (Private Peranakan Museum)
description: A private Peranakan home museum opened by passionate collector Alvin Yapp, featuring over 3,000 rare Baba-Nonya antiques and artefacts in a beautifully decorated Joo Chiat shophouse. Guided tours offer an intimate Peranakan cultural experience.

name: Chinaman Scholar Gallery (Geylang)
description: A fascinating private collection of Straits-born Chinese culture artefacts, antique furniture, and porcelain displayed in a restored Geylang shophouse. A hidden gem for those interested in the Peranakan and Straits Chinese story.

name: Cathedral of the Good Shepherd – Heritage Gallery
description: Beyond the main cathedral, the heritage gallery within the Cathedral of the Good Shepherd traces the 200-year history of the Catholic Church in Singapore with rare photographs, documents, and religious artefacts.

name: Battle Box (WWII Command Centre)
formatted_address: Cox Terrace, Fort Canning Park, Singapore 179616
description: Singapore's best WWII underground museum featuring newly expanded galleries with the largest collection of WWII artefacts from the Malayan Campaign, oral history recordings, and immersive recreations of the 1942 fall of Singapore.

name: Jurong Lake District (Urban Transformation Zone)
description: Singapore's next major economic hub being developed along Jurong Lake, featuring new science museum, parklands, and the 2026 Jurong Region MRT infrastructure. Currently offers scenic lake views, cycling, and the Chinese-Japanese Gardens.

name: Singapore Oceanarium at RWS (Reopening 2025)
description: The reimagined Singapore Oceanarium at Resorts World Sentosa, formerly S.E.A. Aquarium, now featuring enhanced exhibits including a vast open ocean gallery, a coral restoration lab, and themed zones exploring river-to-reef ecosystems.

name: Sungei Road Thieves Market (Historical)
description: The site of Singapore's legendary Sungei Road flea market (1930-2017), once the oldest and largest flea market in Southeast Asia. While the market is closed, the Rochor-Sungei Road district retains its eclectic blend of old Singapore streetscape and art deco shophouses.

name: Lee Kong Chian Natural History Museum (NUS)
description: Singapore's only natural history museum housed at NUS, featuring an extraordinary 50,000-specimen collection including three complete sauropod dinosaur skeletons from the late 19th century, rare minerals, and Southeast Asian biodiversity specimens.

name: Institute of Contemporary Arts (LASALLE)
description: The public gallery of LASALLE College of the Arts in Bugis, showcasing contemporary art exhibitions by students, alumni, and invited regional artists. Free admission makes it a great culture stop in the Bugis arts precinct.

name: Cable Car – HarbourFront Station
description: The HarbourFront departure point for the Singapore Cable Car, offering gondola rides across Keppel Harbour to Sentosa. Features the new Jewel Box attraction at Mount Faber and a Sky Dining option where meals are served mid-air.

name: Siloso Point (Sentosa Extreme Sports)
description: The adventure sports cluster at the western tip of Sentosa featuring AJ Hackett Sentosa bungy jumping (47 metres), Giant Swing, and Vertical Skywalk above the Strait of Singapore. The most extreme outdoor thrills in Singapore.

name: Wavehouse Sentosa
description: A beach sports and entertainment venue at Siloso Beach featuring a FlowRider double wave machine for surfers and bodyboarders, beach volleyball courts, and a casual restaurant. A great way to experience surfing without ocean waves.

name: Mandai Wildlife Reserve Campus
description: The integrated wildlife reserve campus connecting Singapore Zoo, Night Safari, River Wonders, and the new Bird Paradise. The campus features the Mandai Wildlife Bridge, butterfly garden, and a free shuttle bus between attractions.

name: Rochester Park (One-North Lifestyle)
description: A cluster of colonial black-and-white bungalows in Rochester Park converted into restaurants and private clubs, offering al fresco dining in a lush garden setting. Popular with biomedical and tech professionals from the adjacent one-north district.

name: Gillman Barracks (Contemporary Art Galleries)
description: A cluster of international contemporary art galleries in 19 beautifully restored 1930s colonial military barracks at Labrador Park, forming an art enclave with rotating exhibitions from globally significant artists and local talent.

name: Wessex Estate (Colbar Area)
description: A beautifully preserved enclave of colonial black-and-white bungalows near Colbar restaurant in Holland Road, home to artists, craftspeople, and young entrepreneurs. The Colbar canteen has operated since 1953 serving old-school British Hainanese food.

name: Singapore Night Festival (Bras Basah-Bugis Area)
description: An annual outdoor night art festival held in August illuminating the heritage buildings, streets, and museums of the Bras Basah-Bugis arts district with large-scale light projections, performances, and free outdoor installations.

name: Labrador Secret Passage (WWII Tunnels)
description: A partially accessible WWII-era military tunnel and gun battery carved into the Labrador cliffs in 1939 to defend the Strait of Singapore. The visible remains of the 6-inch gun emplacement and ammunition rooms are open for self-guided exploration.

name: Kranji Marshes
description: One of Singapore's best birdwatching sites, a vast freshwater marsh at the western Kranji Reservoir hosting over 170 bird species including the rare Purple Heron, Painted Stork, and Watercock. Features observation towers and a 2-km boardwalk.

name: Coney Island Park – Bicycle Route
description: An 8-km bicycle loop around Coney Island's quiet beaches and forested trails, accessible from Punggol or Lorong Halus. The island's undisturbed grassland, seashore, and forest are home to wild otters, monitor lizards, and over 80 bird species.

name: Plaza Singapura – Level 7 (Entertainemnt)
description: The top floors of Plaza Singapura feature a large Golden Village cinema multiplex, UNIQLO flagship, and a variety of family-friendly entertainment including an indoor play area and casual dining options.

name: Funan – Rooftop Urban Farm & Climbing Wall
description: The rooftop of Funan mall features Singapore's first mall-based urban farm growing herbs and vegetables for the mall's restaurants, alongside an indoor vertical climbing gym spanning multiple floors. A unique destination in the civic district.

name: Biopolis (one-north Science Hub)
description: A biomedical research cluster in one-north with seven interconnected buildings linked by sky bridges, housing world-class research institutes. The outdoor public spaces with cafes and art installations are open to visitors for a glimpse into Singapore's biotech ambitions.

name: Marine Cove (East Coast Park)
description: A casual al fresco lifestyle dining hub at East Coast Park with McDonald's, A&W, café outlets, BBQ stations, and a large children's playground. The go-to F&B stop for cyclists and beachgoers in the eastern coastal park.

name: Parkland Green (East Coast Park)
description: A cluster of casual waterfront restaurants near East Coast Park's popular seafood centre, offering al fresco dining with sea views. Popular with families for grilled seafood, Western food, and cold beverages after cycling.

name: River Valley Road (Zion Rd) Food Cluster
description: The Zion Road hawker precinct is one of Singapore's better-kept secrets for local food in the central area. Multiple hawker stalls serve outstanding local classics including char kway teow, satay, and chwee kueh near the Singapore River.

name: Serangoon North Ave 4 Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in Serangoon North serving the large residential estate with diverse food options. Known for its robust claypot rice, Teochew muay (porridge), and affordable breakfast spreads.

name: The Woodleigh Mall
description: A new community mall in Bidadari estate above Woodleigh MRT, anchored by FairPrice Finest and offering a curated mix of F&B, lifestyle stores, and a rooftop garden. Serving the newly completed Bidadari HDB town.

name: Sengkang Grand Mall
description: A comprehensive integrated community hub in Sengkang featuring NTUC FairPrice, a cinema, a library, a polyclinic, and the Sengkang Sports Centre, connected to Buangkok MRT. One of Singapore's newest integrated town centres.

name: Choa Chu Kang Lot 1 Food Court
formatted_address: 21 Choa Chu Kang Ave 4, Basement, Singapore 689812
description: The basement food court of Lot One Shoppers' Mall in Choa Chu Kang, offering a comprehensive range of affordable local hawker food including CCK-famous ngoh hiang, Western food, and local beverages.

name: Compassvale Crescent Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in the heart of Sengkang estate, serving the large community with a diverse mix of Chinese, Malay, Indian, and mixed hawker food at very affordable prices.

name: Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade (Event Space)
description: The scenic 3.5-km waterfront promenade encircling Marina Bay, hosting Singapore's major outdoor events including National Day fireworks, New Year's Eve countdown, Singapore Grand Prix street circuit, and the Marina Bay food and music festivals.

name: Sentosa Express Monorail
description: A free automated monorail connecting three stations within Sentosa (Beach, Waterfront, Imbiah) and linking to VivoCity and the Sentosa MRT station. The most convenient way to get around Sentosa without a car or bus.

name: Dinosaurs Alive! (Mandai)
description: An outdoor dinosaur-themed experience at the Mandai wildlife campus featuring over 80 life-size animatronic dinosaurs scattered along jungle trails. A fun family add-on to the zoo visit for children who love prehistoric creatures.

name: East Coast Park Bicycle Rental (Xtreme Skate)
formatted_address: East Coast Park Service Road, Singapore 449876
description: Multiple bicycle and rollerblade rental kiosks along East Coast Park, the most accessible outdoor recreation at the beachside parkway. Tandem bikes, children's bikes, and adult cycles available hourly for exploring the full 15-km coastal route.

name: Original Sin (Dempsey Hill)
description: Singapore's most celebrated vegetarian and vegan restaurant, serving creative Mediterranean cuisine in a cosy Chip Bee Gardens shophouse. A long-time favourite in the Holland Village-Dempsey food scene for over 20 years.

name: Lower Pierce Reservoir Park
description: A serene reservoir park bordering primary rainforest in central Singapore, featuring a 1.5-km boardwalk through mature trees, a mangrove inlet, and excellent birdwatching with resident kingfishers and hornbills.

name: Punggol Northshore District
description: Singapore's newest waterfront residential district at the tip of Punggol, featuring sky gardens, parks connector, sea views towards Pulau Ubin, and a pioneer coastal promenade. A showcase of Singapore's future sustainable housing design.

name: Bedok Swimming Complex
description: A popular public swimming complex in Bedok with a 50-metre Olympic pool, learner pools, and a wading pool for children. One of Singapore's most frequented public pools due to its convenient location above Bedok MRT.

name: Buangkok Hawker Centre (Sengkang)
description: A lively hawker centre in the Buangkok estate serving the large northeast residential community with excellent chicken rice, nasi lemak, char kway teow, and Indian food at very affordable prices.

name: Causeway Point Civic & Community Hub
description: The civic and community hub above Causeway Point in Woodlands integrating the Woodlands Civic Centre, a public library, multiple community clubs, and the Singapore Sports Hub Woodlands branch in one convenient integrated facility.

name: Upper Boon Keng Road Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre in the Boon Keng area serving the HDB community with diverse local food. Known for its excellent fish porridge, laksa, and daily dim sum at very affordable prices.

name: Chui Huay Lim Teochew Cuisine (Orchard)
description: Singapore's most esteemed Teochew restaurant, operated by the famous Chui Huay Lim Club since 1990. Renowned for authentic cold crab, braised duck in plum sauce, Teochew steamed pomfret, and traditional yam paste (orh nee) dessert.

name: Jade (The Fullerton Hotel)
formatted_address: 1 Fullerton Square, The Fullerton Hotel, Singapore 049178
description: An award-winning Chinese fine dining restaurant at The Fullerton Hotel, offering exquisite Cantonese cuisine including signature Peking Duck (advance order), dim sum, and seasonal specialties in a heritage colonial setting.

name: Bedok 603 Coffeeshop (Famous Satay)
description: A legendary coffeeshop cluster near Bedok Reservoir known for its outstanding charcoal-grilled satay, oyster omelette, and BBQ sambal stingray. A beloved east Singapore institution for evening hawker dining with an open-air atmosphere.

name: XO Fish Head Bee Hoon (Alexandra)
description: A Michelin Bib Gourmand hawker stall in Aljunied specialising in the Teochew-style milky white fish head bee hoon (rice vermicelli) soup. The rich, collagen-rich broth is made from scratch daily and sells out by noon.

name: Hajah Maimunah Restaurant (Jalan Pisang)
description: A Michelin-recommended Malay-Muslim restaurant famous for its extensive nasi padang spread with over 40 dishes, including the exceptional rendang and lemak siput (spicy cockles in coconut gravy). An institution of authentic Malay cuisine near Bugis.

name: Rochor Centre Wet Market & Food
description: The wet market and hawker floor within the demolished Rochor Centre complex has moved to Jalan Besar area, maintaining the spirit of this historic community market. The area's remaining heritage shops, provision stores, and coffee shops are local institutions.

name: Bedok Mall
description: A comprehensive community mall above Bedok MRT, Singapore's busiest station, featuring FairPrice Finest, Golden Village cinema, and a wide selection of F&B and lifestyle retail serving the large Bedok residential population.

name: Changi Village Hawker Centre
description: A picturesque hawker centre in the rustic Changi Village, famous for its exceptional nasi lemak at Changi Village Nasi Lemak and a wide variety of Malay and Chinese food. Close to the bumboat jetty to Pulau Ubin.

name: East Coast Recreation Centre
description: A recreation hub within East Coast Park offering beach volleyball courts, water sport equipment rentals (kayaks, canoes, pedal boats), bicycle hire, and BBQ pit rentals. One of the most popular weekend outdoor activity centres in eastern Singapore.

name: Hougang Avenue 10 Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre at the base of Hougang Mall serving the northeast community with diverse hawker food including popular Hokkien mee, carrot cake, and economic rice stalls.

name: Farmway Road Market (Kranji)
description: A rustic rural market near the Kranji farms and agri-food belt, selling fresh farm produce directly to consumers. Features mushrooms, vegetables, prawns, and free-range eggs from nearby Singapore farms at reduced prices.

name: Bistro Du Vin (Orchard Hotel)
description: A classic French bistro within the Orchard Hotel, serving traditional Provençal cuisine including bouillabaisse, steak frites, and French charcuterie. A sophisticated dining option on upper Orchard Road with an excellent wine list.

name: Island Creamery (Serene Centre)
description: Singapore's beloved home-grown artisan ice cream shop making creative local-flavoured scoops including Milo Dinosaur, Chendol, Tiger Beer, and Durian flavours. A nostalgic institution in the Holland-Bukit Timah heartland.

name: Nassim Hill Bakery (Nassim Road)
description: An acclaimed artisan bakery and café in the Nassim-Tanglin area known for exceptional sourdough, croissants, house-made granola, and daily-changing pastries. A favourite among the embassy community and upscale Orchard-Tanglin residents.

name: 40 Hands Coffee (Tiong Bahru)
description: A pioneering specialty coffee shop in Tiong Bahru Estate that helped spark Singapore's third-wave coffee scene. Known for exceptional single-origin espresso, flat whites, and house-made breakfast items in a cosy shophouse setting.

name: Timbre+ (one-north)
description: An innovative hawker centre concept in the one-north tech district combining traditional hawker stalls with modern food entrepreneurs, craft beer, live music, and a spacious outdoor park setting. A model for the future of Singapore's hawker culture.

name: Dhoby Ghaut Green
description: A green park at the convergence of Somerset, Orchard, and Dhoby Ghaut MRT stations, featuring regular outdoor events, food trucks, live concerts, and a peaceful escape from the surrounding shopping malls on weekday evenings.

name: Queensway Shopping Centre
description: Singapore's oldest purpose-built shopping centre (1973), the mecca for sports shoes and football jerseys with over 50 sports retail outlets offering authentic and replica jerseys, sports bags, and athletic shoes at competitive prices.

name: Tanglin Shopping Centre
description: A charming 1970s boutique mall on Tanglin Road specialising in Asian antiques, art galleries, Persian carpets, and bespoke tailoring. A favourite of Singapore's expat and art collector community for unique finds.

name: Jumbo Seafood (Riverwalk Tampines)
description: The Tampines Riverwalk outlet of Singapore's most famous chilli crab restaurant, offering the signature mud crab in sweet-savoury chilli sauce alongside black pepper crab and prawn dishes in a spacious setting near East Singapore.

name: Colbar (Wessex Estate)
description: A legendary 1950s British military canteen preserved in Wessex Estate, serving no-frills British Hainanese food including shepherd's pie, pork chops, and traditional kaya toast. Open-air seating under old rain trees in a colonial garden.

name: Kranji Countryside Farmers' Market
description: A vibrant weekend farmers' market at Singapore's Kranji agri-food zone, where local farms sell fresh vegetables, eggs, frog legs, crocodile meat, and artisan products directly to consumers at farm-gate prices.

name: Marine Parade Public Library
description: A public library in Marine Parade offering a comprehensive collection of books and digital resources. Features dedicated children's sections, study spaces, and regular community events and storytelling sessions.

name: JCube (Jurong East)
description: A former ice-skating mall in Jurong East, JCube is being redeveloped as part of the greater Jurong Lake District transformation. The site's Olympic-size ice rink was a unique recreation destination in western Singapore.

name: Bukit Merah View Market & Hawker Centre
description: A bustling market and hawker centre serving the Bukit Merah estate. Known for its variety of affordable Chinese, Malay, and Indian food options, with particularly good roasted meats and a solid wet market downstairs.

name: ActiveSG Sport Centre Network
description: Singapore's network of public sport centres offering swimming pools, gymnasiums, sports halls, and tennis courts at affordable prices managed by Sport Singapore. The Sports Hub is the flagship, with 24 sport centres islandwide.

name: Thomson Road Medical & Heritage Precinct
description: The Thomson Road corridor near Novena MRT is lined with Singapore's largest medical cluster (Tan Tock Seng, Mount Elizabeth Novena) alongside heritage-listed Yean Chuan Thong temple and the beautiful Thomson Road canal.

name: Pasir Panjang Power District (Heritage)
description: A conservation area featuring the former Pasir Panjang Power Station (1952) with its distinctive chimneys and generator halls, earmarked for adaptive reuse as a heritage lifestyle district similar to London's Battersea Power Station.

name: Alexandra Road Food Trail
description: A stretch of Alexandra Road from ABC Brickworks Market to Alexandra Village Food Centre is considered one of Singapore's best hawker corridors, with acclaimed stalls and multiple award-winning food destinations within walking distance.

name: HarbourFront Ferry Terminal (Batam/Bintan Gateway)
description: The gateway to Indonesia's Riau Islands, with multiple daily high-speed ferry services to Batam and Bintan departing every hour. Just 45 minutes to Batam for shopping, seafood, and spa experiences, or 80 minutes to Bintan for beach resorts.

name: Marina Bay Sands – Event Plaza (Outdoor)
description: The outdoor plaza fronting Marina Bay Sands hosts Singapore's largest waterfront events including the F1 Night Race street circuit, New Year Eve celebrations, and the spectacular annual i Light Singapore art festival.

name: The Star Theatre (Buona Vista)
formatted_address: 1 Vista Exchange Green, #01-1 The Star Vista, Singapore 138617
description: A state-of-the-art 5,000-seat performing arts theatre within The Star Vista mall at Buona Vista, hosting major concerts, musical productions, comedy acts, and corporate events. Singapore's second-largest indoor performing venue.

name: Goodwood Park Hotel (Heritage)
description: A National Monument and iconic luxury hotel established in 1929, famous for its grand Tudor-style tower and being Singapore's most beloved high tea venue. The Durian Fiesta at Goodwood Park attracts visitors from across Asia every durian season.

name: Satay by the Bay – Punggol Version (Kopitiam Waterway)
description: A waterfront kopitiam cluster along Punggol Waterway serving local hawker favourites with scenic views over the linear park. A convenient pit stop for cyclists, families, and residents exploring the Punggol Waterway trail.

name: Tanglin Halt Community Club (Heritage Facade)
description: A conservation-gazetted community club building in Tanglin Halt, one of Singapore's first public housing estates. The estate's distinctive 1960s Singapore Housing Board architecture and the preserved coffeeshop cluster along Commonwealth Drive are heritage gems.

name: Mount Faber & Keppel Hill Viewpoint
description: The multiple lookout points and Keppel Hill pavilion around Mount Faber offer some of Singapore's finest views over Keppel Harbour, the world's busiest port, Sentosa, and the southern islands. Sunrise and sunset are spectacular from the hilltop.

name: NTU Heritage Campus (Yunnan Garden)
description: The historic Nanyang University campus at NTU features stunning 1950s southern Chinese architecture including the former Nantah Library (now NTU Administration Building), Yunnan Garden, and the Nanyang University Arch. Free to explore.

name: Admiralty Park Mega Playground
description: Singapore's largest themed outdoor children's playground spread over multiple hills in Admiralty Park, with 880 metres of interconnected adventure play equipment through the forest. Free admission and accessible from Admiralty MRT.

name: Woodlands Town Garden
description: A community garden and park in Woodlands town offering cycling paths, a fitness corner, a butterfly enclosure, and a small pond. Popular with Woodlands residents for morning exercise and family outings.

name: Jurong West St 61 Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre serving the far western Jurong West community near Pioneer MRT with affordable local hawker food. Known for reliable economic rice, fish soup, and local breakfast options.

name: Queenstown Stadium (Old Site) Community Space
description: The former Queenstown Stadium site, Singapore's first HDB-era sports facility, now repurposed as an open green community space in the Queenstown estate. The surrounding heritage blocks of 1960s HDB flats form Singapore's oldest public housing neighbourhood.

name: Pasir Ris Central Hawker Centre
description: A comprehensive hawker centre at the heart of Pasir Ris town serving the northeast community with diverse local dishes including popular satay, Indian mee goreng, and Chinese dim sum. Easily accessible above Pasir Ris MRT.

name: Bishan-Ang Mo Kio Park – Community Node
description: The Community Node section of Bishan-AMK Park features Singapore's first sustainable naturalised river channel, a water playground, floating wetlands, and riverside dining. A benchmark of Singapore's award-winning biophilic urban design.

name: Tuas Second Link Recreation Area
description: The coastal parkland adjacent to the Tuas Second Link bridge to Malaysia offers Singapore's best views of the Western Causeway and Johor Strait. A remote park popular with anglers and birdwatchers in far western Singapore.

name: Toa Payoh Dragon Playground (Heritage)
description: Singapore's most beloved heritage HDB playground built in 1979, featuring a large concrete dragon sculpture that generations of Singapore children grew up playing on. Listed for conservation by NHB as a living HDB heritage landmark.

name: Night Safari – Creatures of the Night Show
description: A free amphitheatre wildlife show within Night Safari featuring live nocturnal animals including owls, fishing cats, and civets in a 20-minute educational performance. One of the highlights of the Night Safari experience.

name: Singapore Zoo – Jungle Breakfast with Wildlife
formatted_address: 80 Mandai Lake Road, Singapore Zoo, Singapore 729826
description: The most memorable dining experience in Singapore – a buffet breakfast at Singapore Zoo's Jungle Breakfast venue while free-ranging orangutans, macaques, and park animals wander through the surrounding rainforest.

name: Sentosa Golf Club – Serapong Course
description: One of Asia's most prestigious golf courses at Sentosa, home to the Singapore Open on the European Tour. The Serapong Course combines breathtaking sea views with challenging fairways along Sentosa's southern coastline.

name: Tanah Merah Ferry Terminal (Bintan Ferries)
description: The main departure point for ferries to Bintan Island Resort, Indonesia. Operates multiple daily crossings to Bintan's luxury beach resorts (Banyan Tree, Aman, Club Med) and the Bintan duty-free shopping zone.

name: Clarke Quay Central
description: A compact mall directly above Clarke Quay MRT, housing a range of affordable restaurants, bakeries, a hair salon cluster, and a NTUC FairPrice. Convenient hub for the Clarke Quay-Chinatown corridor.

name: Esplanade Mall Food Court
description: A waterfront food court inside the Esplanade Mall with a variety of Asian and local food at accessible prices, perfect for a quick meal before or after a performance at the Esplanade Theatres on the Bay.

name: Resorts World Sentosa Casino Membership Perks
description: The dining landscape within Resorts World Sentosa hosts Michelin-starred restaurants including Forest by Indranee, Feng Shui Inn, and the Hard Rock Cafe Singapore, alongside the Malaysia Boleh hawker-style food court at Resorts World.

name: One Fullerton (Waterfront Restaurants)
description: A cluster of premium waterfront restaurants and bars facing the Marina Bay waterfront adjacent to Merlion Park, including Odette, Jumbo Group, and the popular Jaan sky dining. Some of Singapore's finest dining with unrivalled Marina Bay views.

name: Changi Airport Terminal 1 – Heritage Zone
description: Terminal 1 of Changi Airport features a charming heritage zone with restored 1980s terminal décor, a social history gallery of aviation, and the famous Terminal 1 orchid garden. Free for visitors without a boarding pass.

name: Dhoby Ghaut MRT – Three-Line Interchange
description: Singapore's only three-MRT-line interchange station at Dhoby Ghaut connecting the North-South, Circle, and North-East lines. Features a retail podium, information centre, and serves as the gateway to Orchard Road and the Singapore Art Museum.

name: Geylang East Public Library
description: A public library in Geylang East serving the east Singapore community with books, digital resources, and community programmes. Features dedicated sections for children, teens, and seniors along with study spaces.

name: Maritime Experiential Museum (RWS Sentosa)
description: An immersive maritime heritage museum within Resorts World Sentosa chronicling the maritime Silk Road and Southeast Asian seafaring history. Features a typhoon simulator, replica 16th-century dhow, and interactive exhibits on Singapore's port heritage.

name: Esplanade Library (Arts Library)
description: A specialist arts library within the Esplanade building with an outstanding collection of performing arts resources including rare music scores, theatre scripts, and a media archive. Open to the public with free admission.

name: Singapore Tyler Print Institute
description: A not-for-profit contemporary art space at Robertson Quay dedicated to the art of print-making, housing a print studio and gallery featuring Singapore and international artists' unique limited-edition prints and paperworks.

name: Singapore DUCKtours (City & Marina Route)
description: An amphibious vehicle tour of Singapore's iconic landmarks via road and the Marina Reservoir, offering a unique perspective of the city from both land and water. Departs from Suntec City and covers Marina Bay, Padang, and the historic civic district.

name: Yong Siak Street (Tiong Bahru Café Row)
description: The café-lined street at the heart of Tiong Bahru's trendy café scene, with independent bookshop BooksActually, Forty Hands coffee, Plain Vanilla bakery, and PS Café all within a few shophouse blocks. Singapore's most photogenic café row.

name: Eminent Frog Porridge (Geylang Lor 24A)
description: Singapore's most celebrated frog porridge restaurant at Geylang, serving the signature dish of tender frog meat in a ginger and spring onion sauce alongside smooth white porridge. A quintessential late-night Singapore dining adventure.

name: Golden Peony (Conrad Centennial)
description: An award-winning Cantonese restaurant at Conrad Centennial Hotel near Marina Bay, famous for its outstanding dim sum lunch and signature dishes including wasabi prawns and crispy roasted pork. A favourite for business lunches.

name: Marina Mandarin Singapore (Atrium)
description: The Marina Mandarin hotel features a stunning 21-storey open atrium designed by John Portman that was once Asia's largest. The glass-enclosed atrium and lobby lounge offer a dramatic architectural experience near Marina Square.

name: Chinatown Food Discovery Walk (Guided)
description: A popular food and heritage walking tour starting at Chinatown MRT, visiting Maxwell Food Centre, Chinatown Heritage Centre, Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, and hidden coffee shops and traditional bakeries in the conservation area.

name: East Coast Kayak & Watersports Hub
formatted_address: 1000 East Coast Parkway, S1 Car Park, Singapore 449876
description: Kayak and paddleboard rental operations along East Coast Park offering guided kayaking tours of the coastal waters, a stand-up paddleboard hire, and beginner sailing courses. A popular water sports hub for eastern Singapore.

name: Lower Delta Road Hawker Centre
description: A neighbourhood hawker centre near Tiong Bahru serving a mix of local food including popular ban mian (flat wheat noodles), popiah, and Indian mee goreng. Accessible from Tiong Bahru and Bukit Merah MRT.

name: Rider's Café (Bukit Timah)
description: A charming outdoor café within Saddle Club grounds adjacent to Bukit Timah Nature Reserve, offering all-day breakfasts, Western mains, and cakes in a tranquil stable-side garden setting. Horses can sometimes be seen training nearby.

name: Geylang Malay Heritage District Walk
description: A self-guided heritage walk through the Geylang Lorong district exploring Singapore's largest Malay residential community, with traditional Malay houses, halal food lanes, mosques, and the Geylang Serai cultural precinct.

name: Kallang Theatre
description: A 1,740-seat theatre at the Sports Hub precinct, one of Singapore's oldest performing arts venues (opened 1973) hosting Chinese opera, local theatre, stand-up comedy, and dance performances. A heritage performing arts institution.

name: Punggol Point Park
description: The northernmost tip of Punggol, featuring a small park with a lookout over the Strait of Johor and Pulau Ubin. Anglers, cycling enthusiasts, and photography lovers frequent this peaceful spot for sunrise views and nature sightings.

name: Lorong Chuan Area Hawker Cluster
description: A neighbourhood hawker cluster near Lorong Chuan MRT serving the Serangoon-Bishan corridor with diverse local food including popular yong tau foo, nasi padang, and prawn noodles at affordable prices.

name: Pasir Panjang Wholesale Centre (Market)
description: Singapore's largest wholesale fresh produce market, operating in the early morning hours when restaurants, hotels, and hawkers stock up on fresh vegetables, seafood, and poultry. Public visitors can buy in bulk at wholesale prices from midnight onwards.

name: Bedok South Market & Food Centre
description: A neighbourhood market and hawker centre in Bedok South offering good-value local food with popular prawn mee, nasi lemak, and Malay kueh. A reliable community food hub for residents of the Bedok South estate.

name: Ang Mo Kio Central Market & Food Centre
description: The main market and hawker centre in the heart of AMK town, offering a wet market and a large selection of hawker stalls. Known for solid chicken rice, carrot cake, and char kway teow at consistently good prices.

name: Singapore-Johor Causeway Heritage Viewpoint
description: The Singapore side of the 1924 Johor-Singapore Causeway, the second-busiest land border crossing in the world. The viewing areas offer a symbolic perspective on Singapore-Malaysia relations and the daily flow of cross-border workers.

name: Shenton Way & Anson Road Food Cluster
description: The compact Tanjong Pagar-Shenton Way food circuit hosts some of Singapore's most-loved lunchtime stalls including the legendary Ah Weng Koh Hainan Tea, char siew shops, and a cluster of excellent hawker and zi char options serving the CBD.
